docs.title: Phase Execution Checklist
docs.text: Mark each task ⬜ → ✅ as completed. Update matrix row Status column in parallel.

docs.title: Phase 0 — Pre-flight ⬜
docs.text:
• ⬜ Read the full Superplan
  • ⬜ Read the full Matrix
  • ⬜ Read this guide completely
  • ⬜ cd modules/control-panel/frontend
  • ⬜ Rename the current src/ to src_REFERENCE/
  • ⬜ Create a new clean src/
  • ⬜ Run grep -r "studyId" src_REFERENCE/ --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx" -l and save output as baseline
  • ⬜ Run grep -r "backtestSessionId" src_REFERENCE/ --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx" -l and save output as baseline

docs.title: Phase 1 — Delete Dead Files ⬜
docs.text:
These files have no remaining consumers after the refactor. Delete them first so they do not confuse import tracing in later phases.

  • ⬜ components/DashboardCard.tsx
  • ⬜ components/IndexPage.tsx
  • ⬜ app/globals.css
  • ⬜ app/page.module.css
  • ⬜ app/backtests/backtests.css
  • ⬜ app/trainings/detail/page.tsx (and folder app/trainings/detail/)
  • ⬜ app/trials/detail/page.tsx (and folder app/trials/detail/)
  • ⬜ app/experiments/detail/page.tsx (and folder app/experiments/detail/)
  • ⬜ components/client-pages/ExperimentDetailClient.tsx
  • ⬜ components/dashboard/experiments/detail/ExperimentDetailPage.tsx
  • ⬜ components/dashboard/trainings/list/jobs/TrainingJobsTable.module.scss
  • ⬜ lib/backtests/helpers/index.ts
  • ⬜ styles/index.scss — also remove its import from styles/globals.scss

Verification: git status shows only the deleted files. No other changes.

docs.title: Phase 2 — Pure Moves (Keep / Move / Rename, no content changes) ⬜
docs.text:
Process all matrix rows with Decision = Keep, Move, Rename, or Move+Rename (no Split, no Merge). File content is not modified — only path changes. After moving each file, mark the matrix row ✅.

In clean-build mode, these are copy/create operations from src_REFERENCE/ into the new src/ target path, not in-place filesystem moves.

Route folder renames ([studyId] → [trainingId]) are handled in Phase 5, not here. Do not process those rows in this phase.

After moving each batch of files, run the import update strategy below for that batch. Do not defer all import fixes to the end.

docs.title: Import update strategy (apply after each file move)
docs.text:
# For each file moved from OLD_PATH to NEW_PATH:
grep -r "OLD_PATH" src/ --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx" -l
# Then update each found file to import from NEW_PATH instead

After completing all moves in this phase:

# Sweep for any remaining stale old paths
grep -r "client-pages/" src/ --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx"
grep -r "hooks/dashboard/" src/ --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx"
grep -r "components/dashboard/shared/" src/ --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx"

Verification: No broken import paths remain for moved files. grep sweeps return empty.

docs.title: Phase 3 — Execute Splits ⬜
docs.text:
For each Split row:

  1. Create a backup copy of the source file in an _OLD/ location if you want an additional local safety checkpoint
  2. Read the entire source file
  3. Create the target files (both the renamed primary and all new secondary files from the New-File Manifest above)
  4. Move logic to each new owner, copying all comments line-for-line
  5. Delete or hollow out the source (if it is not itself a target)
  6. Mark matrix row ✅
  7. Run import update strategy for the new paths

In clean-build mode, read source files from src_REFERENCE/ and create all targets under the new src/.

Split execution order (dependencies first):

  • ⬜ lib/backtests/constants.ts → lib/backtests/constants.ts + lib/charts/constants.ts
  • ⬜ lib/backtests/helpers/api.ts → lib/backtests/listParams.ts + lib/shared/parseDataResponse.ts
  • ⬜ lib/dashboard/api.ts → 6 new api.ts files (must happen before hook/component splits that depend on API calls)
  • ⬜ components/dashboard/shared/formatters.ts → lib/shared/formatters.ts + lib/shared/derivedMetrics.ts
  • ⬜ components/dashboard/shared/table/Table.tsx → Table.tsx + TableDensityToggle.tsx + useTableDensityToggle.ts
  • ⬜ components/charts/ChartPanel.module.scss → ChartPanel.module.scss + ChartScaleSelector.module.scss
  • ⬜ components/charts/ChartPanel.tsx → ChartPanel.tsx + useChartPanel.ts + ChartScaleSelector.tsx + hooks/charts/useChartScaleSelector.ts
  • ⬜ components/charts/ChartLegendBox.tsx → ChartLegendBox.tsx + useChartLegendBox.ts
  • ⬜ components/charts/ChartToolbox.tsx → ChartToolbox.tsx + useChartToolbox.ts
  • ⬜ components/dashboard/shared/table/cells/TrialActionsCell.tsx → TrialActionsCell.tsx + TrialInLaunchAction.tsx
  • ⬜ hooks/backtests/useBacktestData.ts → useBacktestData.ts + useBacktestDetail.ts
  • ⬜ components/dashboard/backtests/detail/viewer/BacktestDashboard.tsx → BacktestDashboard.tsx + useBacktestDashboard.ts
  • ⬜ components/dashboard/backtests/detail/viewer/BacktestCharts.tsx → BacktestChartsPanel.tsx + useBacktestChartsPanel.ts
  • ⬜ components/dashboard/backtests/detail/viewer/BacktestTradeBrowser.tsx → BacktestTradeBrowser.tsx + useBacktestTradeBrowser.ts
  • ⬜ components/dashboard/backtests/detail/viewer/BacktestMetrics.tsx → BacktestMetrics.tsx + BacktestReasonMetadata.tsx
  • ⬜ components/dashboard/trials/detail/TrialDetailPage.tsx → TrialDetailPage.tsx + PredictionHealthPanel.tsx + PredictionHealthPanel.module.scss
  • ⬜ components/dashboard/trainings/detail/TrainingDetailPage.tsx → TrainingDetailPage.tsx + hooks/trainings/useTrainingData.ts
  • ⬜ components/dashboard/experiments/list/ExperimentsListPage.tsx → ExperimentsListPage.tsx + ExperimentsFilters.tsx + ExperimentsFilters.module.scss + useExperimentsFilters.ts
  • ⬜ components/dashboard/experiments/detail/charts/markers.ts → lib/experiments/charts.ts + lib/experiments/predictionMarkers.ts
  • ⬜ components/dashboard/experiments/detail/charts/ExperimentChartsPanel.module.scss → split scss
  • ⬜ components/dashboard/experiments/detail/charts/ExperimentChartsPanel.tsx → ExperimentChartsPanel.tsx + useExperimentChartsPanel.ts + ExperimentChartsToolbar.tsx + ExperimentParquetOverlays.tsx
  • ⬜ components/dashboard/experiments/detail/charts/CustomExperimentChartPanel.tsx → CustomExperimentChartPanel.tsx + useCustomExperimentChartPanel.ts
  • ⬜ components/dashboard/experiments/detail/charts/features/ExperimentFeaturesExplorer.tsx → ExperimentFeaturesExplorer.tsx + useExperimentFeaturesExplorer.ts
  • ⬜ components/dashboard/experiments/detail/charts/parquets/ParquetPlotBuilder.tsx → ParquetPlotBuilder.tsx + useParquetPlotBuilder.ts
  • ⬜ components/dashboard/experiments/detail/pages/ExperimentChartsPage.tsx → ExperimentChartsPage.tsx + hooks/experiments/useExperimentData.ts
  • ⬜ components/HitlPage.tsx → HitlPage.tsx + useHitlPage.ts (also wrap in AdminShell — see AF-5)
  • ⬜ components/dashboard/ExecutiveHomePage.tsx → ExecutiveHomePage.tsx + useExecutiveHomePage.ts
  • ⬜ styles/backtests.scss → styles/backtests.scss + styles/charts-shared.scss (see Phase 8)
  • ⬜ styles/dashboard.scss → styles/dashboard.scss + styles/experiments-panels.scss (see Phase 8)

docs.title: Phase 4 — Execute Merges ⬜
docs.text:
Process the three merge pairs (see Merge Pairs Warning at the top of this guide).

  • ⬜ Backtests launch merge: useBacktestsLaunchForm.ts (primary) absorbs useBacktestsLaunchPanel.ts → target useBacktestLaunchPanel.ts
  • ⬜ Trainings launch merge: useTrainingLaunchPanel.ts (surviving hook) absorbs useTrainingLaunchForm.ts form state → target useTrainingLaunchPanel.ts
  • ⬜ Trials launch merge: useBacktestLaunchForm.ts (primary) absorbs useTrialBacktestLaunchPanel.ts (wrapper) → target useTrialBacktestLaunchPanel.ts

After each merge: update imports, delete the absorbed secondary source, mark both rows ✅.

docs.title: 5a — Route folder renames
docs.text:
In Next.js, dynamic segments are folders, not files.

If you are using clean-build mode, create the canonical target folders directly under the new src/app/... tree and do not recreate legacy [studyId] or [backtestSessionId] folders.

If you are using in-place mode instead, use git mv to rename the folder:

# Top-level trainings
git mv "app/trainings/[studyId]" "app/trainings/[trainingId]"

# Nested: experiments > trainings
git mv "app/experiments/[experimentId]/trainings/[studyId]" \
       "app/experiments/[experimentId]/trainings/[trainingId]"

# Backtests
git mv "app/backtests/[backtestSessionId]" "app/backtests/[backtestId]"

# Nested: experiments > backtests
git mv "app/experiments/[experimentId]/backtests/[backtestSessionId]" \
       "app/experiments/[experimentId]/backtests/[backtestId]"

docs.title: 5b — Content renames inside TypeScript files
docs.text:
After folder renames, search for remaining occurrences:

grep -r "studyId" src/ --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx" -l
grep -r "backtestSessionId" src/ --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx" -l
grep -r "study_id\|StudyId\|studyIdFromPath" src/ --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx"
grep -r "backtestSession\|BacktestSession" src/ --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx"

Common files that will have hits: hook parameters, API function arguments (getTraining(studyId) → getTraining(trainingId)), path context return values (studyIdFromPath → trainingIdFromPath), URL path builder calls, filter query param keys, generateStaticParams implementations.

Mark all relevant matrix rows ✅ as you complete each rename.

docs.title: Phase 6 — Apply U-Marks ⬜
docs.text:
These are behavioral code changes. Each requires modifying or creating code, not just moving files. Each must be implemented and verified before marking ✅.

  • ⬜ U1 — ListBacktestSessionsQuery in types/backtests.ts must extend BaseListQuery with page, page_size, search fields

  • ⬜ U2 — listBacktestSessions() in lib/backtests/api.ts (after split) must return PaginatedResponse<T> instead of a raw array

  • ⬜ U3 — hooks/backtests/useBacktestDetail.ts must wrap useDetailResource + getBacktestSession, symmetric with useExperimentDetail, useTrainingDetail, useTrialDetail. Already created in Phase 3 — verify implementation here.

  • ⬜ U4 — ExperimentsFilters already created in Phase 3 — verify it is wired into ExperimentsListPage and follows the same pattern as BacktestsFilters, TrainingsFilters, TrialsFilters

  • ⬜ U5 — Naming normalization: handled in Phase 5. Verify no studyId remains.

  • ⬜ U6 — Each entity hook that sets a default sort must export its default sort config as a named constant (e.g., DEFAULT_BACKTEST_SORT, DEFAULT_TRAINING_SORT, etc.) rather than hardcoding inline

  • ⬜ U7 — All list page hooks must use pathId ?? parsedFilter (nullish coalescing) for context resolution. Grep for ternary patterns in useBacktestsListPage, useTrainingsListPage, useTrialsListPage, useExperimentsListPage and normalize

  • ⬜ U8 — All list page hooks must return the same base shape: { page, pageSize, filters, sort, context, data, setters, goToPage }. Compare all four and align divergences.

  • ⬜ U9 — Verify all four entities have XxxFilters.tsx + useXxxFilters.ts after Phase 3.

  • ⬜ U10 — In lib/trainings/api.ts (after split): rename getTraining(studyId) parameter to getTraining(trainingId). Update all callers.

  • ⬜ U11 — In hooks/shared/usePathContext.ts (renamed from useDashboardPathContext): return trainingIdFromPath not studyIdFromPath. Update all consumers.

  • ⬜ U12 — isXxxContextRoute flags must be calculated inside usePathContext and returned as part of its contract, not scattered across individual list page hooks.

docs.title: Phase 7 — Final Import Update Pass ⬜
docs.text:
After all moves, splits, and merges are complete, do a final sweep to catch any remaining stale import paths.

# Stale shared paths
grep -r "from.*dashboard/shared" src/ --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx"
grep -r "from.*client-pages" src/ --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx"
grep -r "from.*hooks/dashboard" src/ --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx"
grep -r "from.*lib/dashboard" src/ --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx"
grep -r "from.*utils/api" src/ --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx"
grep -r "from.*utils/value" src/ --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx"
grep -r "from.*lib/backtests/helpers" src/ --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx"
grep -r "from.*components/charts/" src/ --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx"

Each result is a broken import. Update to the target path from the matrix Proposed Target column and mark ✅.

Verification: All grep sweeps return empty results.

docs.title: Phase 8 — SCSS Global Style Cleanup ⬜
docs.text: Both backtests.scss and dashboard.scss use body.page-* body-class scoping and cannot be converted to CSS Modules. The goal is to extract rules that belong to different owners.

docs.title: 8a — Create styles/charts-shared.scss (from backtests.scss) ⬜
docs.text:
These class families appear under BOTH body.page-backtests AND body.page-experiments in the current backtests.scss and are shared chart infrastructure:

# Find candidate shared sections in backtests.scss
grep -n "page-experiments\|overview-scrollbar\|overview-range\|overview-shade\|overview-handle\|chart-legend\|charts-unified" \
  src/styles/backtests.scss

Move those rule blocks (combined body.page-backtests, body.page-experiments selectors and standalone shared chart sections) to styles/charts-shared.scss. Import charts-shared.scss from styles/globals.scss.

docs.title: 8b — Create styles/experiments-panels.scss (from dashboard.scss) ⬜
docs.text:
All body.page-experiments blocks in dashboard.scss are experiments-owned panel styling, not dashboard-owned. Move them:

# Find all body.page-experiments blocks in dashboard.scss
grep -n "body.page-experiments" src/styles/dashboard.scss

Based on current analysis, these are approximately lines 251–390 and cover: .panel, .panel-header, .panel-body, .panel-toggle, .panel-action, .panel-collapsed, .resolution-selector, .resolution-button families.

Move these blocks to styles/experiments-panels.scss. Import from styles/globals.scss.

docs.title: 8c — Dedup: remove class names already in .module.scss files ⬜
docs.text:
After extraction, find any class that exists in both a global SCSS file and a component .module.scss file — the component-scoped version wins, remove from global:

# Extract all class names from backtests.scss
grep -oP '\.[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_-]+' src/styles/backtests.scss | sort -u > /tmp/backtests_classes.txt

# Find which of those also appear in .module.scss files
while IFS= read -r cls; do
  result=$(grep -r "${cls}" src/components/ --include="*.module.scss" -l 2>/dev/null)
  [ -n "$result" ] && echo "$cls found in: $result"
done < /tmp/backtests_classes.txt

For each hit: if the component-scoped version fully covers the use case, remove the rule from the global SCSS file.

docs.title: Phase 9 — Final Verification ⬜
docs.text:
• ⬜ All 288 matrix rows marked ✅
  • ⬜ All tasks in this guide marked ✅
  • ⬜ grep -r "studyId\|backtestSessionId\|StudyId\|BacktestSession" src/ returns no TypeScript hits
  • ⬜ grep -r "from.*dashboard/shared\|from.*client-pages\|from.*hooks/dashboard\|from.*lib/dashboard" src/ returns empty
  • ⬜ grep -r "from.*utils/" src/ --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx" returns empty
  • ⬜ No // @ts-ignore, /* eslint-disable */, or suppression pragmas introduced
  • ⬜ Final file tree matches CONTROL_PANEL_FRONTEND_REFACTOR_SUPERPLAN_FINAL_FILE_TREE.txt
  • ⬜ git diff --stat shows no unexpected files outside modules/control-panel/frontend/src/

docs.title: Quick Reference: Matrix Status Legend
docs.text: Symbol Meaning ⬜ Not started 🔄 In progress ✅ Completed ⚠️ Blocked or needs review

docs.title: Control Panel Frontend Refactor — Super Plan
Decision Update — Study Naming
docs.text: On 2026-03-24, the canonical naming decision was revised. study / studies / studyId now supersede the earlier training / trainings / trainingId frontend-target wording in this plan. This supersession exists because the real project domain, database, model-trainer, and control-panel backend are already study-centric (OptunaStudy, study_id, study_name). Any instruction in this document that previously pushed the opposite rename direction must be read as obsolete and replaced by the canonical study naming decision.

docs.title: Status
docs.text:
Approved for execution. All 283 source files audited. Target tree verified. U-marks defined.

This document is the canonical architecture reference for the refactor. The only per-file execution source of truth is CONTROL_PANEL_FRONTEND_REFACTOR_SUPERPLAN_FINAL_TREE_MATRIX.md.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
• Project area: modules/control-panel/frontend/src/
  • Total source files: 283
  • Nature of the refactor: This is NOT a cosmetic rename/move. It is a deep structural and behavioral refactor that reorganizes the entire frontend architecture while simultaneously creating new code, new patterns, and new shared infrastructure.

docs.title: What this refactor includes
docs.text:
1. Folder restructure — file moves, renames, deletes per the File Action Matrix (283 rows)
  2. Splits creating NEW files — ~20+ new hook files extracted from components (useHitlPage, useExecutiveHomePage, useBacktestDashboard, useBacktestChartsPanel, useBacktestTradeBrowser, useTrainingData, useExperimentData, useExperimentChartsPanel, useChartLegendBox, useChartPanel, useChartToolbox, useExperimentFeaturesExplorer, useParquetPlotBuilder, useBacktestDetail, ExperimentsFilters, PredictionHealthPanel, etc.)
  3. New shared infrastructure — usePathContext (replaces useDashboardPathContext), useResolvedId (replaces useResolvedDashboardId), useQuery (replaces useDashboardQuery)
  4. New filter pattern — ExperimentsFilters extracted for symmetry (U4/U9). All entities get XxxFilters.tsx + useXxxFilters.ts
  5. Naming normalization — trainingId→studyId, backtestSessionId→backtestId across routes, params, hooks, API calls
  6. Data-fetch pattern unification (U1-U12) — new code: backtest query extends BaseListQuery (U1), backtest list returns PaginatedResponse (U2), new useBacktestDetail hook (U3), sort defaults as exported constants (U6), unified context resolution pathId ?? parsedFilter everywhere (U7), uniform list page hook return contract (U8), API parameter naming normalization (U10), path context returns trainingIdFromPath (U11), context flags unified in shared hook (U12)
  7. Route context vs Filter context system — new behavioral contract enforced across all list pages, filters, and launch forms
  8. Merges — hooks being absorbed (useTrainingLaunchForm→useTrainingLaunchPanel, useBacktestsLaunchPanel→useBacktestLaunchPanel, useTrialBacktestLaunchPanel absorbed)
  9. Import rewrites — done during/after moves

docs.title: What this refactor does NOT include
docs.text:
• No new features or screens
  • Backend contract alignment may be required when public route or API naming still exposes the superseded training terminology
  • No changes to external API contracts

docs.title: Annexes
docs.text:
This plan references two companion artifacts that live alongside it:

  1. File Action Matrix — CONTROL_PANEL_FRONTEND_REFACTOR_SUPERPLAN_FINAL_TREE_MATRIX.md
  2. 283 rows, one per source file
  3. Each row: current file path, decision (Keep/Move/Rename/Split/Merge/Delete), proposed target path(s), reason, reviewed status

  4. This is the actionable source of truth for every file-level transformation

  5. Target File Tree — CONTROL_PANEL_FRONTEND_REFACTOR_SUPERPLAN_FINAL_FILE_TREE.txt

  6. Complete target folder structure with all concrete files
  7. Derived from the matrix; must remain synchronized
  8. Delete targets are excluded; SCSS split targets (charts-shared.scss, experiments-panels.scss) are already part of the final tree and must remain synchronized during execution

docs.title: L1. Same role, same pattern
docs.text: If two entities fulfill the same role, they must use the same folder pattern, naming pattern, and responsibility split.

docs.title: L2. Same role, same naming
docs.text: Equivalent hooks, pages, components, and lib files must use parallel names across entities.

docs.title: L3. Split by responsibility
docs.text:
• app/ — routing only
  • components/ — rendering only
  • hooks/ — React state and behavior only
  • lib/ — pure logic only
  • types/ — cross-cutting contracts only
  • styles/ — global styles only

docs.title: L4. No page components mixed with UI primitives
docs.text: Page shells, reusable UI primitives, and Next dynamic wrappers are different roles and must be separated.

docs.title: L5. No pure logic inside components/
docs.text: Parsers, builders, constants, toggle state models, requirements extractors, and config definitions must live in lib/.

docs.title: L6. Shared means truly shared
docs.text: Only code reused by more than one entity may live in shared areas.

docs.title: L7. Chart infrastructure and chart domain logic are different layers
docs.text: Shared chart infrastructure → components/ui/charts/, hooks/charts/, lib/charts/. Entity-specific chart logic → components/{entity}/detail/charts/, hooks/{entity}/, lib/{entity}/.

docs.title: L8. Entity hooks must be explicit
docs.text: Hooks under hooks/{entity}/ should carry the entity prefix whenever the role is entity-specific.

docs.title: L9. Remove redundant dashboard/ path level
docs.text: The whole application is already the dashboard. The extra prefix only adds noise.

docs.title: L10. Replace client-pages naming
docs.text: Those files are lazy client wrappers, not page implementations. They become loaders/.

docs.title: L11. Route trees must be complete
docs.text: If an entity supports list and detail routes with ids, the route tree must show every intermediate level and id explicitly.

docs.title: L12. Folder tree approval comes before file-level execution
docs.text: No file moves are approved until the folder tree itself is approved. (DONE — tree is approved.)

docs.title: Locked naming decisions
docs.text:
• Target architecture uses study / studies / studyId. Prior training / trainings / trainingId wording is superseded.
  • Target architecture uses backtest / backtests / backtestId. No backtestSession naming in target.
  • Entity corridors are plural: experiments/, studies/, trials/, backtests/
  • Collection pages stay plural, detail pages stay singular
  • Launch capability: components/<entity>/launch/ with singular naming (BacktestLaunchPanel, not BacktestsLaunchPanel)
  • One component = one colocated hook (C1). No separate form hooks surviving alongside panel hooks.

docs.title: File colocation rules
docs.text:
• Page owner: Page.tsx + usePage.ts + Page.module.scss — colocated under components/pages/{entity}/{list|detail}/
  • Feature owner: Panel.tsx + usePanel.ts + Panel.module.scss — colocated under components/{entity}/{capability}/
  • Filter owner: Filters.tsx + useFilters.ts + Filters.module.scss — colocated under components/{entity}/list/filters/

docs.title: The distinction
docs.text:
The frontend must distinguish between:

  • Route context — the user is inside a parent entity page hierarchy (e.g., /experiments/:experimentId/trainings)
  • Filter context — the user is on a global collection page narrowing results via query params (e.g., /trainings?experiment_id=10)

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
1. When route context exists: show contextual header, lock/hide parent filters, do not pretend context came from a free search filter
  2. Filter context decides search narrowing only, not page chrome
  3. Effective fetch precedence: pathId ?? parsedFilter (nullish coalescing). Path always wins.
  4. UI semantics must preserve the distinction even though data-fetch uses the same resolved ID

docs.title: Affected families
docs.text:
All list page hooks, all filter components, and all launch forms that accept contextual parent IDs must explicitly preserve this split:

  • useBacktestsListPage.ts, useTrainingsListPage.ts, useTrialsListPage.ts
  • BacktestsFilters.tsx, TrainingsFilters.tsx, TrialsFilters.tsx, ExperimentsFilters.tsx
  • Launch forms that accept contextual parent IDs

docs.title: Unification Marks (U1–U12)
docs.text: These marks define the data-fetch pattern normalization that must be applied across all four entities during or after the structural refactor. They ensure symmetric, predictable behavior.

docs.title: U1 — Backtest query type extends BaseListQuery
docs.text: ListBacktestSessionsQuery must extend BaseListQuery with page, page_size, search like the other entities instead of using a bespoke shape.

docs.title: U2 — Backtest list API returns PaginatedResponse
docs.text: listBacktestSessions() must return PaginatedResponse<T> (with total, page, page_size) instead of a raw array, matching experiments/trainings/trials.

docs.title: U3 — Create useBacktestDetail hook
docs.text: Extract backtest session detail loading from useBacktestData into a thin useBacktestDetail.ts wrapper (wraps useDetailResource + getBacktestSession), symmetric with useExperimentDetail, useTrainingDetail, useTrialDetail. Already in target tree.

docs.title: U4 — Create ExperimentsFilters component
docs.text: Extract inline filter UI (search + status inputs) from ExperimentsListPage.tsx into ExperimentsFilters.tsx + useExperimentsFilters.ts + .module.scss for structural symmetry with backtests/trainings/trials. SYMMETRY IS MORE IMPORTANT THAN AVOIDING EXTRACTION OF SIMPLE COMPONENTS. Already in target tree.

docs.title: U5 — Rename studyId → trainingId everywhere
docs.text: All routes [studyId] → [trainingId]. All params, hooks, API calls, path context. No study naming survives in the target frontend.

docs.title: U6 — Sort defaults as exported constants per entity
docs.text: Each entity should export its default sort configuration as a named constant rather than hardcoding inline in hooks.

docs.title: U7 — Unified context resolution pattern
docs.text: All entities must use pathId ?? parsedFilter (nullish coalescing) for context resolution. Eliminate trainings' inconsistent ternary pattern that diverges from the other three entities.

docs.title: U8 — Uniform list page hook return contract
docs.text: All list page hooks must return the same shape: { page, pageSize, filters, sort, context, data, setters, goToPage }. Entity-specific fields are additive, not structural deviations.

docs.title: U9 — Every entity has XxxFilters + useXxxFilters
docs.text: Every filter component follows the XxxFilters.tsx + useXxxFilters.ts pattern. No inline filters in page components.

docs.title: U10 — API parameter naming normalization
docs.text: getTraining(studyId) → getTraining(trainingId). All API client functions use canonical entity names, not legacy Optuna names.

docs.title: U11 — Path context returns trainingIdFromPath
docs.text: useDashboardPathContext (→ usePathContext) returns trainingIdFromPath not studyIdFromPath. All consumers updated.

docs.title: U12 — Context flags unified in shared path context hook
docs.text: isXxxContextRoute flags are calculated in the shared path context hook (usePathContext), not scattered across individual list page hooks.

docs.title: Key Audit Findings (Actionable)
docs.text: These findings emerged during the systematic audit and carry user-annotated priority. All are resolved in the matrix.

docs.title: AF-1 — Experiments filters must be extracted (PRIORITY: HIGH)
docs.text: User annotation: "EXTRAER!!!! SIMETRIA Y ORDEN ES MAS IMPORTANTE!!!" The experiments list page has inline filters while backtests/trainings/trials all have dedicated filter components. This is resolved by U4 and the matrix already has the split target.

docs.title: AF-2 — Lib folder asymmetry: RESOLVED (justified, no action needed)
docs.text: lib/backtests/ has 8 files, lib/experiments/ has 7 files after splits. lib/trainings/ and lib/trials/ have only api.ts each. This asymmetry is justified and correct. Trainings and trials are genuinely simpler entities: no WebSocket, no chart type definitions, no marker transformation pipelines, no domain formatters. Their complexity lives in types/ (cross-cutting contracts) and hooks/ (behavior). Adding lib files for cosmetic symmetry would be wrong.

docs.title: AF-3 — Entity orchestrator hooks: DECIDED
docs.text:
useBacktestData (684 lines) is a massive orchestrator combining session list, dashboard state, chart fetching, and control actions. The asymmetry in size vs other entity hooks is justified (backtests have real-time WebSocket, chart sync, trade navigation). The following orchestrator decisions are already final in the matrix — not pending verification:

  • Backtests: useBacktestData.ts → Split → useBacktestData.ts (stays as pure orchestrator) + hooks/backtests/useBacktestDetail.ts (thin detail wrapper, symmetric with other entities)
  • Trainings: TrainingDetailPage.tsx → Split → TrainingDetailPage.tsx + hooks/trainings/useTrainingData.ts (new orchestrator extracting inline training detail + trials sorting + experiment context logic)
  • Experiments: ExperimentChartsPage.tsx → Split → ExperimentChartsPage.tsx + hooks/experiments/useExperimentData.ts (new orchestrator extracting inline experiment detail + requirements + features + chart state logic)
  • Trials: No orchestrator extraction needed — useTrialDetailPage.ts already exists as the page hook

docs.title: AF-4 — Trainings detail: simpler but still gets orchestrator extraction
docs.text: Trainings detail page is simpler than other entities (no chart viewer, no prediction health). This is NOT an asymmetry bug — trainings are genuinely simpler entities. However, the matrix does mandate a split: TrainingDetailPage.tsx → Split+Move → components/pages/trainings/detail/TrainingDetailPage.tsx + hooks/trainings/useTrainingData.ts. The orchestrator extracts inline training detail loading, trials sorting, and experiment context resolution — consistent with React best practices (components = presentation, hooks = logic) and symmetric with the other entity orchestrator pattern (AF-3).

docs.title: AF-5 — HitlPage must be wrapped in AdminShell
docs.text: HitlPage currently does NOT use AdminShell — it renders standalone without the standard navigation bar. During refactor, HitlPage must be wrapped in AdminShell so HITL gets the same navigation chrome as all other pages. This is a behavioral change (new wrapper), not just a move.

docs.title: Matrix ⚠️ Audit Notes (Complete Reference)
docs.text:
The matrix contains 7 ⚠️ audit notes. All are listed here so the refactor agent does not miss any:

  1. HitlPage + AdminShell — HitlPage must be wrapped in AdminShell during refactor (see AF-5 above)
  2. useTableDensity → useTableDensityToggle — C1 rename for symmetry with TableDensityToggle.tsx
  3. BacktestCharts → BacktestChartsPanel — C1/L2 rename for symmetry with useBacktestChartsPanel.ts and ExperimentChartsPanel.tsx
  4. TrialDetailPage.tsx row was missing — Added during audit. Split+Move extracts PredictionHealthPanel to shared UI
  5. TrialDetailPage.module.scss row was missing — Added during audit. Moves with page to page corridor
  6. useTrainingLaunchForm merge — Form state logic merges INTO useTrainingLaunchPanel.ts (C1: one component = one hook)
  7. useTrainingLaunchPanel surviving hook — This is the surviving colocated hook that absorbs useTrainingLaunchForm.ts

docs.title: Target Folder Structure Summary
docs.text:
The complete file-by-file tree is in the annex CONTROL_PANEL_FRONTEND_REFACTOR_SUPERPLAN_FINAL_FILE_TREE.txt.

High-level target structure:

src/
├── __tests__/
├── app/                          # Routes only (thin wrappers)
│   ├── backtests/
│   ├── experiments/              # Deep nesting: trainings/trials
│   ├── hitl/
│   ├── trainings/
│   └── trials/
├── components/
│   ├── backtests/                # Entity feature: detail/charts, launch, list/filters
│   ├── experiments/              # Entity feature: detail/charts/config/shared, list/filters
│   ├── loaders/                  # Client-only dynamic wrappers (ex client-pages)
│   ├── pages/                    # Page owners: backtests/experiments/trainings/trials
│   ├── trainings/                # Entity feature: launch, list/filters
│   ├── trials/                   # Entity feature: launch, list/filters
│   └── ui/                       # Shared: cards, charts, context, layout, pagination, panel,
│                                 #         prediction-health, status, table, table-density, tree
├── constants/
├── hooks/
│   ├── backtests/                # Entity data hooks
│   ├── charts/                   # Shared chart hooks
│   ├── experiments/              # Entity data hooks
│   ├── shared/                   # useDetailResource, usePaginatedResource, usePathContext, etc.
│   ├── trainings/                # Entity data hooks
│   └── trials/                   # Entity data hooks
├── lib/
│   ├── api.ts                    # Shared transport
│   ├── apiUrls.ts                # URL builders
│   ├── backtests/                # Entity lib
│   ├── charts/                   # Shared chart logic
│   ├── experiments/              # Entity lib
│   ├── parquets/                 # Parquet API
│   ├── shared/                   # formatters, paths, valueGuards, etc.
│   ├── trainings/                # Entity lib
│   └── trials/                   # Entity lib
├── styles/                       # Global SCSS
└── types/                        # Cross-cutting type contracts

docs.title: Preferred execution mode
docs.text:
• Do not treat the current src/ tree as the place to refactor in place
  • Preserve the current tree as src_REFERENCE/
  • Assemble the final architecture in a new clean src/
  • Read legacy sources from src_REFERENCE/; create final targets under src/
  • Temporary intermediate breakage is acceptable during execution; the required guarantee is a correct final result that passes final verification

docs.title: Order of operations
docs.text:
See CONTROL_PANEL_FRONTEND_REFACTOR_IMPLEMENTATION_GUIDE.md for the full phase-by-phase checklist with ⬜/✅ tracking per task.

  1. Delete dead files first — IndexPage, DashboardCard, orphaned stylesheets, legacy detail routes, barrel index
  2. Create new directories — all target folders that don't exist yet
  3. Move simple files (Keep/Move/Rename) — bulk of the 283 files, no content changes. Mark each matrix row ✅ as completed.
  4. Execute splits — files that produce multiple targets. ⚠️ CRITICAL: Splits create NEW files that do not appear as source rows in the matrix. When the Decision column says Split, read the Proposed Target column carefully — every +-separated path after the first is a NEW file you must create. Back up source files to _OLD/ first.
  5. Execute merges — hooks being absorbed. ⚠️ CRITICAL: Three merge groups exist where TWO source rows share ONE target. See the Merge Pairs Warning below — process the primary file first, then absorb the secondary into it. Never create the target file twice.
  6. Apply naming normalization — studyId→trainingId, backtestSessionId→backtestId. In Next.js, dynamic segment renaming means renaming the folder (e.g., [studyId]/ → [trainingId]/), not just the page.tsx file inside it. Use git mv for folder renames. Then search all .ts/.tsx files for the legacy identifiers — see studyId Rename Scope below.
  7. Apply U-marks — data-fetch pattern unification (U1-U12). These are behavioral code changes with concrete file-level tasks listed in the implementation guide. They are NOT simple moves — each requires modifying or creating code.
  8. Update all imports — After each phase (not only at the end), search for old import paths and update them. Strategy: for each moved file, run grep -r "<old-path>" src/ to find all consumers, then update each import to the new path. Verify with a final grep -r sweep for any remaining old paths. The matrix Proposed Target column is your reference for every new path.
  9. SCSS global style cleanup — extract shared chart-infrastructure rules from backtests.scss, extract shared panel rules from dashboard.scss (see concrete targets below)

docs.title: Agent Non-Negotiable Rules
docs.text: These rules are extracted from AGENTS.md and apply to every single action during this refactor. Violations of these rules have caused real damage in past sessions.

docs.title: R1. Read the full file before editing
docs.text: Before touching any file — for a Split, Merge, or content change — read the entire file first. Understand its structure, naming conventions, and all existing comments. Do not start editing without this step.

docs.title: R2. Comments are absolutely protected
docs.text:
• NEVER delete existing comments, docstrings, or commented-out code during moves
  • NEVER rewrite, shorten, paraphrase, or relocate comments — copy them line-for-line to their equivalent location in the target
  • NEVER "clean up" or simplify code that is outside the scope of the current operation
  • Before finishing any Split or Merge, review the diff specifically for removed comment lines and restore any that were lost
  • If a refactor makes an existing comment look outdated or misplaced, STOP and ask before changing it

docs.title: R3. Scope discipline
docs.text: When moving a file: touch only that file and its direct import references. Do not refactor unrelated code "while you're at it".

docs.title: R4. No error suppressors
docs.text: Never use // @ts-ignore, // @ts-expect-error, /* eslint-disable */, or any suppression pragma. If TypeScript raises an error after a move, fix the root cause.

docs.title: R5. Create _OLD/ backup before large rewrites
docs.text: For every Split and Merge operation, keep a backup copy before making changes. Any consistent _OLD/ location is acceptable; there is no required standard path.

docs.title: R6. Explain before executing
docs.text: Before starting each phase, state what you are about to do, which files will be affected, and what the expected result is. Do not execute without this pre-announcement.

docs.title: R7. Never commit without explicit instruction
docs.text: Do not run git commit or git push at any point. Only run git for status/diff inspection.

docs.title: Critical rules during execution
docs.text:
• Route context vs filter context split must be preserved — see Section above
  • One component = one hook — no leftover form/panel hook pairs
  • Path always wins — pathId ?? parsedFilter everywhere
  • No study or backtestSession naming in target — clean break
  • All entity patterns must be symmetric — same folder structure, same hook shapes, same naming conventions
  • Comments are protected — see R2 above
  • Create _OLD/ backup before every Split and Merge (see R5 above)

docs.title: Merge Pairs Warning
docs.text:
The matrix has three groups where TWO source rows merge into ONE target file. If you process matrix rows sequentially without knowing this, you will either create the target twice or overwrite the first merge result with the second.

How to process a merge pair:

  1. Process the PRIMARY row first (the one whose logic is the main body of the target)
  2. Read the SECONDARY row's source file and absorb its logic into the target already created in step 1
  3. Delete the secondary source file
  4. Mark both matrix rows ✅
Group Primary row (Move+Rename) Secondary row (Move+Merge) Target Backtests launch useBacktestsLaunchForm.ts → Rename to useBacktestLaunchPanel.ts useBacktestsLaunchPanel.ts → Absorbed into target components/backtests/launch/useBacktestLaunchPanel.ts Trainings launch useTrainingLaunchPanel.ts → Move+Merge (absorbs form) useTrainingLaunchForm.ts → Merge source components/trainings/launch/useTrainingLaunchPanel.ts Trials launch useBacktestLaunchForm.ts → Rename to useTrialBacktestLaunchPanel.ts useTrialBacktestLaunchPanel.ts → Absorbed into target components/trials/launch/useTrialBacktestLaunchPanel.ts

docs.title: studyId Rename Scope
docs.text:
Step 6 (naming normalization) must reach ALL occurrences of studyId and backtestSessionId across the codebase, not only route folder names. After renaming folders, run these searches to find remaining occurrences:

# Find all remaining studyId references
grep -r "studyId" src/ --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx" -l

# Find all remaining backtestSessionId references
grep -r "backtestSessionId" src/ --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx" -l

# Find study* naming in function params and API calls
grep -r "study_id\|studyId\|StudyId" src/ --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx"

Common locations beyond routes: hook parameters, API function arguments (getTraining(studyId)), path context return values (studyIdFromPath), URL path builders, and filter query param keys.

docs.title: SCSS global style split targets (concrete)
docs.text: Both backtests.scss and dashboard.scss use body.page-* class scoping (global page-theme styles, not CSS Modules). They CANNOT be fully converted to component-scoped .module.scss files because they rely on body-class matching. The concrete split strategy:

docs.title: styles/backtests.scss (1371 lines)
docs.text:
• Stays as: styles/backtests.scss — all body.page-backtests-scoped page chrome, layout, panel primitives, session cards, chart controls, trade browser/details, metrics sections
  • Extract to: styles/charts-shared.scss — rules that match BOTH body.page-backtests AND body.page-experiments: legend items/markers, overview-range overlays/shades/handles/lines, overview scrollbar, charts-unified sections. These are shared chart infrastructure, not backtests-owned.
  • During execution: any rules that duplicate what already exists in component .module.scss files are removed from the global file (dedup, not copy)

docs.title: styles/dashboard.scss (660 lines)
docs.text:
• Stays as: styles/dashboard.scss — all body.page-dashboard-scoped admin shell chrome, dashboard grid, stat cards, tables, forms, filters, messages
  • Extract to: styles/experiments-panels.scss — rules that match body.page-experiments: panel/panel-header/panel-body/panel-toggle/panel-action/panel-collapsed, resolution selector/buttons. These are experiments-owned panel styling, not dashboard-owned.
  • During execution: same dedup rule as backtests.scss

docs.title: Archived Artifacts
docs.text:
Previous audit and planning documents have been archived to docs/plans/archived/frontend/. They are historical references only — this super-plan and its annexes are the canonical source of truth.

Archived documents (in docs/plans/archived/frontend/):

  • admin-dashboard-v2.md — backend module map
  • CRITICAL_AUDIT_HOOKS_NAMING.md — hooks naming asymmetry analysis
  • SYSTEMATIC_MATRIX_AUDIT.md — systematic audit issues
  • CONTROL_PANEL_FRONTEND_FINALIZATION_PLAN.md — earlier finalization rules
  • FRONTEND_ARCHITECTURE_AUDIT.md — original 14-finding architecture audit
  • CONTROL_PANEL_FRONTEND_FOLDER_TREE_REFACTOR_PLAN.md — original 12 laws + file mapping (absorbed into this super-plan)
  • FINAL_TREE_COHERENCE_ISSUES.md — coherence check (empty, all resolved)
  • CONTROL_PANEL_FRONTEND_FINAL_TREE_AUDIT_MATRIX.md — earlier matrix version (pre-audit-fixes)
  • CONTROL_PANEL_FRONTEND_FINAL_TREE_AUDIT_MATRIX.20260323_0205_backup.md — matrix backup
  • FINAL_TREE_TREE.txt — earlier tree version
  • control_panel_frontend_review_workspace/ — working directory (current_tree + final_tree snapshots)

Do not use docs/audits/front/CONTROL_PANEL_FRONTEND_FINAL_TREE_AUDIT_MATRIX.md for execution. It is outdated.

docs.title: Control Panel Frontend Manual Decision Log
docs.text:
Decision update (2026-03-24): canonical frontend naming is now study / studies / studyId. Earlier matrix wording that pushed training / trainings / trainingId as the target naming is superseded by this project-wide decision and must be read as obsolete where it conflicts.

Current file Decision Proposed target Reason Reviewed Status __tests__/TrialDetailPage.test.tsx Keep __tests__/TrialDetailPage.test.tsx Focused trial-detail component test with concrete page-contract assertions; no role mismatch was found. Yes ✅ __tests__/dashboardApi.test.ts Keep __tests__/dashboardApi.test.ts Direct test surface for the dashboard API client; test ownership follows the production module rather than a separate entity folder. Yes ✅ __tests__/time.test.ts Keep __tests__/time.test.ts Dedicated test for chart-time helpers; still valid as a top-level frontend test artifact. Yes ✅ components/DashboardCard.tsx Delete [delete] Only consumer is IndexPage.tsx which is also Delete; no remaining consumers after refactor — dead code. Yes ✅ components/HitlPage.tsx Split+Move components/pages/HitlPage.tsx + components/pages/useHitlPage.ts Real page implementation with polling, theming, and operational sections; this is page-layer code, not a generic component primitive. It contains massive inline state management (9x useState: status, statusError, events, eventsError, logs, logsError, isStatusLoading, isEventsLoading, isLogsLoading) plus 3x fetch functions and useEffect polling interval that should be extracted to useHitlPage hook for React best practices. ⚠️ AUDIT NOTE: HitlPage currently does NOT use AdminShell — must be wrapped in AdminShell during refactor so HITL gets standard navigation bar. Yes ✅ components/IndexPage.tsx Delete [delete] The live root route is already owned by ExecutiveHomeClient, and this older card-based entry page has no remaining local consumers, so the legacy entry surface should be removed rather than preserved in the final tree. Yes ✅ components/Panel.tsx Move components/ui/panel/Panel.tsx Shared collapsible panel primitive reused across multiple dashboard surfaces, so it belongs in shared UI infrastructure. Yes ✅ components/dashboard/AdminShell.tsx Move components/pages/AdminShell.tsx Cross-entity dashboard shell infrastructure used by many page surfaces; the role is page-shell infrastructure, not entity feature logic. Yes ✅ components/dashboard/ExecutiveHomePage.tsx Split+Move components/pages/ExecutiveHomePage.tsx + components/pages/useExecutiveHomePage.ts Cross-entity executive landing page that should move to pages corridor, but it contains inline sorting state for experiments/trainings/trials tables (3x useState + 3x useMemo with sortRows) that should be extracted to useExecutiveHomePage hook for React best practices (components = presentation, hooks = logic). Yes ✅ constants/dashboard.ts Keep constants/dashboard.ts Centralized dashboard list, status, sort, and polling constants; current role still matches a dedicated constants module. Yes ✅ hooks/experiments/useExperimentRequirements.ts Keep hooks/experiments/useExperimentRequirements.ts Experiment-domain resource hook wrapping shared detail loading; role and placement are already coherent. Yes ✅ hooks/usePageTheme.ts Move hooks/shared/usePageTheme.ts Shared DOM-side-effect hook used across many page families, so it belongs under shared hooks rather than root hooks/. Yes ✅ hooks/useSyncedRef.ts Move hooks/shared/useSyncedRef.ts Generic React state/ref helper with no entity ownership; shared hook placement is the coherent role-based destination. Yes ✅ hooks/backtests/useBacktestCharts.ts Move components/backtests/detail/charts/useBacktestCharts.ts This hook is local chart composition support for the backtests charts surface, so under the new colocation rule it should live beside that owner instead of staying in the shared hooks corridor. Yes ✅ hooks/backtests/useBacktestConfig.ts Move+Rename hooks/charts/useChartConfig.ts The config contract is consumed by both backtests and experiments chart flows, so frontend ownership is shared chart configuration infrastructure rather than backtests-only. Yes ✅ hooks/backtests/useBacktestData.ts Split hooks/backtests/useBacktestData.ts + hooks/backtests/useBacktestDetail.ts Split to extract backtest session detail loading into a thin wrapper useBacktestDetail (17 lines, wraps useDetailResource + getBacktestSession) for symmetry with experiments/trainings/trials, while useBacktestData remains as pure orchestrator (session list, dashboard data state, chart fetching, control actions). Yes ✅ hooks/backtests/useBacktestWebSocket.ts Keep hooks/backtests/useBacktestWebSocket.ts Low-level realtime transport hook remains backtests-owned because the socket endpoint and message taxonomy are tied to backtest session semantics. Yes ✅ hooks/backtests/useBacktestWebSocketHandlers.tsx Keep hooks/backtests/useBacktestWebSocketHandlers.tsx Adapter/policy hook that maps backtests socket events into viewer state mutations remains coherently inside the backtests hooks boundary. Yes ✅ hooks/backtests/useChartDataFetcher.ts Move+Rename components/backtests/detail/charts/useBacktestChartDataFetcher.ts This cached range-fetch hook is a local support dependency of the backtests chart owner, so it should be colocated beside that charts corridor while adopting explicit backtest naming. Yes ✅ hooks/backtests/useChartToggles.ts Move+Rename components/backtests/detail/charts/useBacktestChartState.ts This toggle/state hook is local backtests chart UI state, so it should be colocated with the chart owner and renamed to the canonical chart-state form. Yes ✅ hooks/backtests/useMarkerHighlights.tsx Move+Rename components/backtests/detail/charts/useBacktestMarkerHighlights.tsx Marker-highlighting is local support for the backtests chart surface, so it belongs beside that owner rather than in the shared hooks bucket.
