Total: ~55 CLI commands across 12 modules. Every one of these is a potential MCP tool.

docs.title: 4.2 The --emit Flag Pattern
docs.text:
Most pipeline commands have --emit/--no-emit flag (default: False). This controls event emission.

Modules with --emit: data-fetcher, labeling-service, window-preparer, model-trainer, backtesting-engine, housekeeping.

Problem: Default is --no-emit. For the active supervisor, this MUST default to --emit (or always emit).

docs.title: 5. Process Control Matrix — What Can Be Paused/Stopped Today
5.1 Current Capabilities (verified from code)
docs.text: Module Pause Resume Cancel/Stop Status Query Mechanism backtesting-engine YES YES (--resume session_id) YES YES DB flag polling (pause_requested/cancel_requested in BacktestSession) live-trading-runner YES YES YES (stop) YES CLI commands → in-memory instance manager model-trainer NO YES (study-level + per-trial checkpoint) SIGINT/SIGTERM only NO (no CLI cmd) Optuna load_if_exists=True + checkpoint resume + orphan trial cleanup. Stop via OS signal only. data-fetcher NO NO NO NO Fire-and-forget labeling-service NO NO NO NO Fire-and-forget window-preparer NO NO NO NO Fire-and-forget data-processing NO NO NO NO Library only housekeeping NO NO NO NO One-shot operations

docs.title: 5.2 model-trainer — What It Already Has (corrected)
docs.text:
The model-trainer has robust resume at multiple levels:

  • Study-level resume: optuna.create_study(load_if_exists=True) + remaining_trials = n_trials - (completed + pruned) → only runs what's missing
  • Phase-level skip: If all trials in a phase are finalized → "Skipping already completed phase"
  • Per-trial checkpoint resume: --resume flag (default True) → resolve_resume_checkpoint_path() finds latest epoch .pt → load_checkpoint() restores model + optimizer + scheduler + epoch + best metrics
  • Orphan cleanup: On re-entry, cancels any trials left in running state from interrupted runs
  • Graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM: Signal handler → cancels running trials → updates all DB counters → marks study STOPPED

docs.title: 5.3 model-trainer — What's Actually Missing (for active supervisor)
docs.text:
The remaining gaps are narrower than originally stated:

  1. No DB-flag-based stop — Stop requires sending SIGTERM to the OS process. An agent would need to know the PID. Adding stop_requested flag polled in handle_trial_complete callback would let the agent stop via DB write.
  2. No status CLI command — Cannot query progress without reading DB directly. A model-trainer status --experiment-id <n> command would expose counters + current trial info.
  3. No pause — Only full stop exists. Pause (checkpoint current trial + halt without cancelling) would be a new feature.

docs.title: 5.4 What Else Needs Process Control
docs.text: Module What's needed Priority Difficulty model-trainer stop_requested DB flag + callback check CRITICAL Medium model-trainer status CLI command HIGH Easy data-fetcher cancel_requested for long multi-symbol fetches Medium Easy window-preparer cancel_requested for long FAT window builds Medium Easy

docs.title: 5.5 Live Trading Supervision — Deep Audit (CRITICAL)
docs.text:
This is the most critical module for active supervision. Live trading involves real money. Every failure mode must be anticipated and monitored.

Source: modules/live-trading-runner/src/rainbow/live_trading_runner/ (all files read in full)

docs.title: 5.5.1 Control Surface — What Exists Today
docs.text:
The live-trading-runner has the richest control surface of any module. All 8 CLI commands map to Python API methods on LiveTradingRunner:

CLI Command Python Method State Transition What It Does start --execution-plan-id <n> start() → STARTING → RUNNING Load execution plan, create LiveSession DB row, bootstrap model/features/window, start stream stop [--execution-plan-id <n>] stop() → STOPPING → STOPPED Halt stream, persist final state, emit LIVE_TRADING_STOPPED, cleanup loop pause [--execution-plan-id <n>] pause() → PAUSING → PAUSED Halt decision processing, keep stream alive, positions stay open resume [--execution-plan-id <n>] resume() → RESUMING → RUNNING Resume decision processing from cursor checkpoint status [--execution-plan-id <n>] status() (no transition) Return InstanceSnapshot with state, mode, symbol, timeframe, timestamps reload [--execution-plan-id <n>] reload() (no transition) Hot-reload runtime config without restarting session force-close [--execution-plan-id <n>] force_close() (no transition) Queue force-close request; executed on next closed candle tick positions positions() (no transition) Return all open trades across active sessions

State Machine (state_machine.py): Enforces legal transitions via next_state_for_command(). States: STARTING, RUNNING, PAUSING, PAUSED, RESUMING, STOPPING, STOPPED, FAILED. Illegal transitions raise ValueError.

docs.title: 5.5.2 Events Emitted — The Supervisor's Eyes
docs.text:
The live-trading-runner emits 4 event types, more than any other module:

1. LIVE_TRADING_STARTED — Emitted per session on start(). Payload: {execution_plan_id, execution_plan_uuid, live_session_id, mode, target_symbol, target_timeframe}

2. LIVE_TRADING_STOPPED — Emitted per session on stop(). Also emitted with {scope: "no_active_sessions"} when stop is called with nothing running. Payload: {execution_plan_id, execution_plan_uuid, live_session_id, reason}

3. LIVE_ANOMALY_DETECTED — Emitted on two conditions:

  • Data gap auto-pause: When auto_pause_on_data_gap=True and observed candle gap exceeds data_gap_tolerance_candles × timeframe_delta. Payload includes {anomaly_type: "data_gap_auto_pause", observed_gap_seconds, tolerated_gap_seconds}.
  • Stream runtime failure: When stream processing raises an unrecoverable error. Payload includes {anomaly_type: "stream_runtime_failure", details: <error_reason>}. Instance transitions to FAILED state.

4. LIVE_EXEC_SUMMARY — Emitted every metrics_snapshot_every_n_ticks ticks AND on every tick with execution activity (orders executed, positions opened/closed). Payload: {live_session_id, execution_plan_id, symbol, timeframe, decision_id, candle_id, equity, realized_pnl_total, unrealized_pnl_total, open_positions_count, closed_trades_count, executed_orders_count}

This is the primary heartbeat the supervisor should monitor. If LIVE_EXEC_SUMMARY events stop arriving for a session, something is wrong.

docs.title: 5.5.3 Safety Guards Already Implemented
docs.text:
Kill Switch (LiveRuntimeConfig.kill_switch_enabled):

  • Checked at the TOP of process_closed_candle() in runtime_loop.py:527-534
  • When kill_switch_enabled=False → decision processing is completely blocked, candle is silently skipped
  • This is a runtime config field — can be toggled via reload command without restarting

Auto-Pause on Data Gap (LiveRuntimeConfig.auto_pause_on_data_gap):

  • Checked in runtime_loop.py:551-581 after candle ingestion
  • Compares consecutive candle timestamps against data_gap_tolerance_candles × timeframe_delta
  • If gap exceeds tolerance → emits RuntimeControlAction(action="auto_pause_data_gap") → InstanceManager catches it and transitions session to PAUSED + emits LIVE_ANOMALY_DETECTED
  • This is automatic — no supervisor intervention needed for detection, but supervisor should be notified to investigate WHY the gap occurred

Risk Manager Force-Flat (RiskManager.should_force_flat()):

  • Checked in runtime_loop.py:694-719 after regular order generation
  • When risk halt is active (e.g., daily loss exceeded) → ALL open positions are force-closed with exit_reason.code="RISK_FORCE_FLAT"
  • Risk manager state is persisted per-tick: risk_manager_daily_pnl, risk_manager_date, risk_manager_halted, risk_manager_state_json

Force-Close on Specific Position (force_close --position-id <n>):

  • Queued via _pending_force_close_by_execution_plan_id map
  • Executed on the NEXT closed candle tick (not immediately — deterministic)
  • Can target a single position or all positions
  • Generates exit_reason.code="MANUAL_FORCE_CLOSE"

Heartbeat Timeout (LiveRuntimeConfig.heartbeat_timeout_seconds):

  • Configured but enforcement depends on the stream service polling loop
  • Value validated as > 0 at config time

docs.title: 5.5.4 Runtime Audit Trail — What's Persisted Per Tick
docs.text:
Every tick persists structured data into live_sessions.notes.runtime_audit (JSON in DB):

CommandHistoryEntry: {command, timestamp, from_state, to_state} — every control command ever applied. Bounded to last 500 entries.

LiveDecisionEntry: {decision_id, correlation_id, candle_id, candle_timestamp_ms, signal, confidence, requested_orders_count, executed_orders_count, opened_positions_count, closed_positions_count, realized_pnl_delta} — every trading decision. Bounded to last 1,000 entries.

LiveMetricsEntry: {timestamp, equity, realized_pnl_total, unrealized_pnl_total, open_positions_count, closed_trades_count} — portfolio snapshots. Bounded to last 1,000 entries.

LiveCursorCheckpoint: {last_processed_candle_timestamp_ms, processed_decision_ids_tail, pending_orders_count, open_trade_ids} — idempotency checkpoint for resume.

DB-level per-tick persistence: live_sessions.status, live_sessions.risk_manager_daily_pnl, live_sessions.risk_manager_date, live_sessions.risk_manager_halted, live_sessions.risk_manager_state_json.

docs.title: 5.5.5 Execution Modes
docs.text:
Mode Adapter Real Money? Risk LIVE_SIM SimExecutionAdapter NO — simulated fills against market data Low — good for testing LIVE_REAL LiveRealExecutionAdapter YES — real orders to exchange CRITICAL — requires maximum supervision

Both modes use the same LiveInstanceLoop, RiskManager, and BotLogic. The only difference is whether orders are simulated or sent to a real exchange.

docs.title: 5.5.6 What the Active Supervisor MUST Watch
docs.text:
The supervisor must implement these watchdog rules for live trading:

Watch Target Detection Method Action Priority Session heartbeat No LIVE_EXEC_SUMMARY event for > heartbeat_timeout_seconds Alert + investigate (stream dead? exchange down?) CRITICAL Equity drawdown LIVE_EXEC_SUMMARY.equity drops below threshold (% of initial) Auto-pause + alert + HITL for resume decision CRITICAL Daily PnL limit risk_manager_halted=True in DB Alert (RiskManager already halts entries, but supervisor should verify and log) HIGH Anomaly events LIVE_ANOMALY_DETECTED event consumed Log + alert + investigate root cause (data gap? stream failure?) CRITICAL Position count explosion LIVE_EXEC_SUMMARY.open_positions_count exceeds max Auto-pause + alert (possible bug in entry logic) HIGH Stale positions Open positions with no activity for > N candles Alert (position may be stuck without SL/TP) MEDIUM Model degradation Signal distribution shift (too many HOLD signals, or all same direction) Alert + suggest investigation MEDIUM Session stuck in transition Instance in STARTING, PAUSING, RESUMING, STOPPING for > N seconds Alert (possible deadlock or crash) HIGH Multiple sessions same symbol Two sessions trading same symbol:timeframe simultaneously Alert (position conflicts possible) HIGH Kill switch disabled kill_switch_enabled=False detected in config Alert (safety guard bypassed) CRITICAL

docs.title: 5.5.7 Supervisor Intervention Matrix — What It Can Do Today
docs.text: Situation Supervisor Action CLI Command Requires HITL? Emergency stop all trading Stop all sessions live-trading-runner stop YES (destructive) Emergency stop one session Stop specific session live-trading-runner stop --execution-plan-id <n> YES (destructive) Pause for investigation Pause sessions live-trading-runner pause --execution-plan-id <n> No (non-destructive, positions preserved) Resume after investigation Resume paused sessions live-trading-runner resume --execution-plan-id <n> YES (resumes real trading) Force close positions Close all or specific position live-trading-runner force-close [--position-id <n>] YES (destructive, realizes PnL) Disable new entries Toggle kill switch via reload live-trading-runner reload (after config change) YES (blocks all decisions) Check current status Query all instances live-trading-runner status No (read-only) Check open positions Query positions live-trading-runner positions No (read-only)

docs.title: 5.5.8 What's Missing for Full Active Supervision
docs.text:
1. No equity threshold auto-stop — RiskManager handles daily PnL limits, but there's no configurable "stop session if equity drops below X%" at the runtime level. The supervisor must implement this by watching LIVE_EXEC_SUMMARY events.

  2. No session-level PnL alerting — The LIVE_EXEC_SUMMARY event has all the data, but there's no built-in threshold that triggers an alert event. The Event Bridge watchdog must implement this.

  3. No cross-session correlation — Each session is independent. No built-in check for "two sessions trading same symbol" or "total portfolio exposure across sessions". The supervisor must aggregate across sessions.

  4. No automatic recovery from FAILED state — Once a session hits FAILED, it requires manual stop + start to recover. The supervisor could automate retry with exponential backoff for transient failures.

  5. Heartbeat enforcement is passive — heartbeat_timeout_seconds is configured but the CONSUMER of heartbeats must enforce it. The Event Bridge watchdog must check last LIVE_EXEC_SUMMARY timestamp per session.

  6. No structured anomaly classification — LIVE_ANOMALY_DETECTED events are free-form. A severity classification (INFO/WARNING/CRITICAL) and standardized anomaly taxonomy would help the supervisor prioritize.

  7. No DB-flag-based kill switch — Current kill switch is config-driven (requires reload). A kill_switch_requested DB flag polled per-tick would let the supervisor kill instantly via DB write without CLI.

docs.title: 6.1 graph-orchestrator
docs.text:
Source: github.com/manupadillaph/rainbow-graph-orchestrator (all files read via GitHub API)

What it actually implements:

  • workflow.py (23KB): LangGraph StateGraph with 7 nodes, compile_graph(), invoke_graph() with checkpoint persistence (SqliteSaver or MemorySaver), Tier0 pre-processor, per-request counter reset, single-hop debug mode, interrupt wait polling
  • state.py (13KB): AgentState TypedDict with messages, experiment/trial/plan IDs, todos, BudgetStats, RequestCounters, hierarchical build_thread_id() / parse_thread_id()
  • event_bridge.py (20KB): Polls get_unprocessed_events(), resolves thread_id from event context, resumes via langgraph_sdk.Command(resume=...), has knowledge indexing hooks for ANALYST_*, ALL_TRIALS_DONE, LIVE_* events. Has bugs: references undefined event_service variable, indentation error in _resolve_thread_id
  • graph_orchestrator.py (12KB): Main module class with health_check, run_dev (langgraph dev), run_up (Docker), invoke, run_event_bridge, config_summary, get_spend_report
  • agents/director.py (16KB): Central coordinator, binds tools from YAML config, calls LLM, executes tools, parses routing via string matching ("delegate to planner"), per-request limit enforcement, forced final summary on cutoff. Uses KnowledgeService + ContextManager for RAG-augmented prompts
  • agents/base.py (21KB): Base agent with _get_llm() tier resolution, _invoke_with_budget() token tracking, _format_messages() with windowing, system prompt from YAML
  • agents/ (analyst, coder, doc, issues, planner, research): Each ~3KB, inherit from BaseAgent, minimal implementations with tool binding
  • tools/module_triggers.py (13KB): Async tool wrappers that emit *_REQUESTED events and call interrupt(). Tools: request_data_fetch, request_labels, request_fat_windows, request_small_windows, request_training, request_backtest, request_data_pipeline
  • tools/stubs.py (24KB): Stub implementations of ALL tools for testing without real modules
  • tools/mock_workers.py (9KB): Mock external worker that processes events and emits DONE responses
  • tools/knowledge.py (3KB): search_knowledge_base tool wrapping KnowledgeService.hybrid_search()
  • tools/store.py (6KB): LangGraph Store read/write tools
  • tools/execution_plans.py (6KB): Create/update execution plans
  • tools/system.py (4KB): Health check, config tools

Honest assessment: This is well-architected code with clear separation of concerns. The patterns are solid (AgentState, budget tracking, thread hierarchy, tool registry, tier-based LLM selection). But it was written AHEAD of the modules being stable, has never been integration-tested, and has accumulated bugs from 3 months of staleness. The 7-agent design is sound for a mature system but premature for current needs.

docs.title: 6.2 graph-utils
docs.text:
Source: github.com/manupadillaph/rainbow-graph-utils (all files read via GitHub API)

What it actually implements:

  • knowledge/service.py (12KB): KnowledgeService with hybrid search (Chroma + BM25 + Reciprocal Rank Fusion), index_document(), index_code(), index_docs(), index_module(), index_content(), index_path(), text chunking by collection settings
  • knowledge/vector_store.py (7KB): Chroma wrapper with add_documents(), similarity_search(), collection management
  • knowledge/bm25.py (6KB): SQLite FTS5-based BM25 index with add_document(), search()
  • knowledge/embeddings.py (3KB): EmbeddingService wrapping Ollama/HuggingFace sentence-transformers
  • knowledge/indexer.py (2KB): IncrementalIndexer that routes events to appropriate collection indexing
  • knowledge/cli.py (7KB): Typer CLI with search, index-file, index-code, index-docs, health-check
  • benchmark/runner.py (11KB): LLMBenchmark class that tests Ollama models (cold start + warm calls), saves results to config/graph-llm-benchmark.yaml
  • cache/: Decision cache (not fully read, appears minimal)
  • mcp/: MCP server exposing knowledge base (not fully read)

Honest assessment: This is more focused and immediately useful than graph-orchestrator. The hybrid search (Chroma + BM25 + RRF) is a solid architecture. The code follows project patterns (Pydantic, structlog, Config). Dependencies are lighter (chromadb, sentence-transformers vs full langchain stack). The indexer is designed to hook into events. Main concern: 3 months stale, needs compatibility check with current common module.

docs.title: 7. What's Missing for the Active Supervisor
7.1 Event System Overhaul (CRITICAL — foundation for everything)
docs.text:
• Consistent lifecycle: Every step MUST emit STARTED + DONE minimum
  • Validated payloads: Use the existing Pydantic payload schemas at emission time
  • Remove dead types: ~35 EventTypes defined but never emitted
  • Default emit=True: Events MUST be always-on, not opt-in
  • Event documentation: Reference table mapping EventType → emitter → payload → consumer

docs.title: 7.2 Process Control for model-trainer (CRITICAL — enables active supervision)
docs.text:
• stop_requested DB flag on OptunaStudy/Experiment, polled in trial callback
  • status CLI command to query training progress
  • Without this, the supervisor can detect problems but cannot act on them

docs.title: 7.3 Knowledge Base (HIGH — enables research agent)
docs.text:
• graph-utils KnowledgeService can index trial results, configs, backtest outcomes
  • Needs event overhaul first so indexer has reliable event stream
  • MCP server for Copilot/Windsurf integration already exists in graph-utils

docs.title: 7.4 LLM Infrastructure (HIGH — enables all agents)
docs.text:
• Ollama setup + benchmark to select models per tier
  • Token tracking and budget management
  • API key configuration for cloud providers (fallback tiers)

docs.title: Target Architecture
docs.text:
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                      USER INTERFACE                              │
│  Chat (primary) + Control Panel (dashboard) + MCP (IDE agents)  │
│  User talks to Director → Director executes or delegates        │
└──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┘
                           │ user messages + HITL responses
                           ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    ORCHESTRATOR (LangGraph)                      │
│                                                                  │
│  ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────┐        │
│  │ Director │→│ Analyst  │  │ Research │  │ Planner  │        │
│  │ (router) │  │ (metrics)│  │ (KB+web) │  │ (plans)  │        │
│  └──────────┘  └──────────┘  └──────────┘  └──────────┘        │
│                                                                  │
│  Tools: ALL 55 CLI commands via MCP + DB queries + KB search    │
│  Budget: tiered LLM selection, token tracking per agent          │
│  State: AgentState with checkpointing (crash-safe)               │
└──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┘
                           │ tool calls (CLI exec, DB queries, KB search)
                           ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    EVENT BRIDGE (daemon)                          │
│                                                                  │
│  Poll Loop:                                                      │
│    events = get_unprocessed_events()                             │
│    for event:                                                    │
│      → index into Knowledge Base (Chroma + BM25)                │
│      → evaluate watchdog rules (stuck? failing? timeout?)       │
│      → if action needed: invoke Director with event context     │
│      → mark_processed(event)                                    │
│                                                                  │
│  Watchdog Loop (parallel):                                       │
│    → check for stuck processes (no events for N minutes)        │
│    → check DB for experiments/trials exceeding time limits      │
│    → check VRAM/RAM health                                      │
│    → alert Director or act directly (configurable HITL gate)    │
└──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┘
                           │ events
                           ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    COMPUTE MODULES (unchanged)                   │
│                                                                  │
│  data-fetcher → data-processing → labeling-service →            │
│  window-preparer → model-trainer → backtesting-engine →         │
│  live-trading-runner                                             │
│                                                                  │
│  Each module: emit events + respond to DB control flags          │
│  Invoked via: CLI commands (MCP tools) or Python API             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

docs.title: Two Operating Modes
docs.text:
Mode 1: Daemon (primary) — Event Bridge runs continuously, monitors events, invokes Director when intervention needed. User doesn't need to be present.

Mode 2: Chat (interactive) — User talks to Director via chat. Director uses same tools, same agents. Can ask: "What's running?", "Stop that experiment", "Run a new experiment with VIT on BTCUSDT".

Both modes use the SAME LangGraph, SAME agents, SAME tools. The difference is who initiates: an event (daemon) or the user (chat).

docs.title: Agents — 4 Initial
docs.text:
Agent Role Tools LLM Tier Director Routes, decides, executes direct actions ALL pipeline CLIs, stop/pause/resume, DB queries, KB search Tier 3 (Genius) Analyst Interprets metrics, compares trials, detects anomalies DB queries, KB search, write insights to Store Tier 2 (Smart) Research Searches KB, explores patterns, proposes hypotheses KB search, web search, write findings to Store Tier 2 (Smart) Planner Generates experiment configs, proposes plans KB search, create_experiment_config, read analyst summaries Tier 3 (Genius)

Coder/Doc/Issues deferred — Copilot/Windsurf + exposed MCP tools handle those needs today.

docs.title: Key Principles
docs.text:
1. No manual CLI — User talks to chat, agent executes CLIs via MCP tools
  2. Events drive everything — Every module action emits events, events trigger agent evaluation
  3. Active supervision — Watchdog detects stuck/failing processes and intervenes
  4. HITL configurable — Destructive actions (stop, delete) require approval. Queries don't.
  5. Research runs in parallel — Agent can start background research, notify user when done
  6. Knowledge is institutional memory — All experiment results indexed for future queries

docs.title: 8.5 Audit Coverage Status
docs.text: Purpose: Track what has been verified from code vs what remains unaudited. This section is a living checklist — update it as audits progress.

docs.title: What HAS been audited
docs.text:
• 48 emit_event calls across 18 files — grep confirmed all call sites and their EventType values.
  • 70+ EventType enum values — listed and categorized in events.py.
  • All 11 module CLIs — command names and Click signatures read. BUT: only the command surface was audited (what commands exist), NOT whether each command's implementation is complete, correct, or production-ready. Full CLI audit pending.
  • Process control matrix — which modules can be started/stopped/paused/resumed, documented in Section 5.
  • Live-trading-runner deep audit — state machine, runtime loop, safety guards, event emissions, runtime audit trail. Verified from source code (Section 5.5).
  • Model-trainer resume/stop — load_if_exists=True, per-trial checkpoint resume, SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers verified.

docs.title: What has NOT been audited yet
docs.text:
• Event payload consistency — Are all 48 emit_event calls passing Pydantic-validated payloads? Or are some passing raw dicts? Unknown.
  • EventConsumer end-to-end — Does the existing consumer actually work? Who consumes events today? Is there a running daemon? Unknown.
  • CLI implementation completeness — Each CLI command was listed but NOT tested or read end-to-end. Commands may be stubs, incomplete, or buggy. Every CLI across all modules must be audited from implementation to execution.
  • Control-panel backend — What does the control-panel API actually consume or expose? What events does it listen to? Unknown.
  • graph-utils compatibility — Read partially via GitHub API. Not tested against current common module. Deferred.
  • graph-orchestrator quality — Known bugs (undefined vars, indentation errors). Not audited for extractable patterns yet.
  • Section 5.5 is TARGET STATE, not current state — The live trading supervision section describes what the supervisor WILL need. It does NOT mean supervision works today. Phase A (foundation) is a prerequisite.

docs.title: Key clarification
docs.text: Section 9 (Concrete Work Items) describes what needs to be BUILT. Before building, Phase A requires a full audit of ALL modules' events, CLIs, and process control to establish the real baseline. The work plan for Phase A will be a separate formal document in docs/plans/.

docs.title: Phase A: Foundation (events + process control)
docs.text:
1. Event system overhaul: Add missing STARTED/DONE, enforce Pydantic payloads, default emit=True
  2. model-trainer enhancements: Add stop_requested DB flag (polled in callback), status CLI command. Resume already works (study-level + per-trial checkpoint).
  3. Live trading supervision hardening: Add kill_switch_requested DB flag polled per-tick, structured anomaly severity classification on LIVE_ANOMALY_DETECTED, equity-threshold auto-stop watchdog rule in Event Bridge.
  4. Clone repos to _REFERENCE/: graph-orchestrator + graph-utils for reference

docs.title: Phase B: Knowledge Layer
docs.text:
1. Audit + fix graph-utils: Compatibility with current common module, add as submodule
  2. Knowledge indexing pipeline: EventConsumer → KnowledgeService (Chroma + BM25)
  3. Backfill existing data: Index all existing trial.json, training_metrics from DB
  4. MCP server for knowledge: Expose to Copilot/Windsurf/Cascade

docs.title: Phase C: Orchestrator
docs.text:
1. LLM benchmark + configuration: Ollama models, tier selection, budget config
  2. Build orchestrator: Using graph-orchestrator patterns (AgentState, budget, tools, EventBridge)
  3. 4 agents: Director, Analyst, Research, Planner — with all 55 CLI tools as MCP
  4. Event Bridge daemon: Poll events, watchdog rules, invoke Director on anomalies. CRITICAL: must implement live trading watchdog rules from Section 5.5.6.
  5. Chat interface: CLI-based chat to Director → eventually Control Panel integration

docs.title: Phase D: Hardening
docs.text:
1. HITL flows: Approval gates for destructive actions. Live trading actions (stop, force-close, resume) MUST require HITL approval.
  2. Budget tracking: Per-agent token usage, daily limits
  3. Crash recovery: Checkpointer-based state persistence
  4. Testing: Integration tests for event → agent → tool → module flows

docs.title: Brain Foundation Audit — Events, CLIs, and Process Control
docs.text: Status: ✅ STAGE 1 — Audit Completed Started: 2026-03-19 Completed: 2026-03-19 Priority: HIGH

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text:
Full audit of all 13 modules to establish the real baseline before building the AI supervisor (brain). Three audit dimensions per module: (1) event emissions — are they complete, consistent, Pydantic-validated? (2) CLI commands — are implementations complete and production-ready, not just stubs? (3) process control — can the module be started, stopped, paused, resumed programmatically? The output is a verified inventory of what works, what's missing, and what's broken. No code changes in this plan — findings here feed a separate execution plan.

Predecessor research: docs/researchs/BRAIN_ARCHITECTURE_ANALYSIS.md (Sections 3-5, 8.5)

docs.title: Global rules (do not remove)
docs.text:
• README template: All module READMEs must follow docs/README_MODULE_TEMPLATE.md.
  • Read from disk before each iteration: Before starting work, re-open this plan, the relevant docs, and the code you will touch. Reality lives on disk (plans, docs, code), not in cached summaries.
  • Git workflow: Use the MCP GitHub tools and follow docs/GIT-COMMITS.md. Never run git add from the repository root unless explicitly specified in this plan.
  • Respect project docs: All changes must comply with the relevant docs (configuration, code generation, database schema, markdown style, etc.).
  • Language: User responses and AI interpretations in the conversation log may be in Spanish, but the work plan document itself and all generated code/docs MUST be written in English.

docs.title: Project docs (must be re-opened when relevant)
docs.text: Document Description CODE-GENERATION.md Coding standards, quality gates CONFIGURATION.md Configuration system, env vars DATA-STORAGE.md Data directory structure, caching DATABASE-SCHEMA.md Database tables schema EVENTS.md Event types and Event Bridge GIT-COMMITS.md Git workflow, commit conventions MARKDOWN-STYLE.md Markdown formatting rules MASTER-PLAN.md Master Plan PIPELINE-FLOWS.md Pipeline workflow diagrams WORK-PLANS.md Work plan creation workflow

docs.title: Module READMEs (must be consulted when touching a module)
docs.text: Module README backtesting-engine README.md bot-logic README.md common README.md control-panel README.md core README.md data-fetcher README.md data-processing README.md database README.md housekeeping README.md labeling-service README.md live-trading-runner README.md model-trainer README.md window-preparer README.md

docs.title: Plan-specific rules
docs.text:
• Audit only — no code changes. This plan produces findings. Code changes go in a separate execution plan derived from these findings.
  • Per-module granularity. Each module gets its own finding with a standardized audit checklist.
  • Evidence required. Every claim must cite file path and line numbers. No "probably" or "should be."
  • Three dimensions per module:
  • Events: List every emit_event call, verify payload is Pydantic-typed (not raw dict), check for missing STARTED/DONE pairs, verify emit parameter defaults.
  • CLI: Read every Click command implementation end-to-end. Document: what it does, whether it's complete, whether it handles errors, whether it has proper exit codes.
  • Process control: Can it be started/stopped/paused/resumed programmatically (not just via CLI)? Document the Python API surface.
  • Cross-cutting audits. After per-module audits, two cross-cutting findings: (1) EventConsumer/Event Bridge status, (2) control-panel backend consumption.
  • Reference doc: docs/EVENTS.md is the canonical event reference. Flag any discrepancies between that doc and actual code.

docs.title: F1 — common
docs.text:

  • CLI: No modules/common/src/rainbow/common/cli.py module CLI exists (modules/common/src/rainbow/common/__init__.py:1).
  • Process control API: No module-level start/stop/pause/resume API in common.
  • Key gap: common defines event contracts (modules/common/src/rainbow/common/types/events.py:30) but does not provide producer/consumer runtime behavior.

docs.title: F2 — core
docs.text:
• Events: MIGRATION_GENERATED and MIGRATION_APPLIED are emitted in modules/core/src/rainbow/core/services/migration_service.py:41 and :74.
  • Payload typing: Raw dictionaries are passed to repository emits (migration_service.py:40, :73).
  • CLI: Broad and implemented surface in modules/core/src/rainbow/core/cli.py with explicit non-zero exits on most failures.
  • Process control API: Partial control exists in BacktestSessionService pause/cancel requests (modules/core/src/rainbow/core/services/backtest_session_service.py:362, :389), but no dedicated resume command/method.
  • Key gaps: No explicit resume control API; event emission in core is narrow (migration-only).

SI EXISTe BacktestSessionService DEBEN EXISTIR LIVE SESSION SERIVE!!!!! hacer el re factor necesario

docs.title: F3 — database
docs.text:
• Events: CLI emits selected event type via emit-event (modules/database/src/rainbow/database/cli.py:232).
  • Payload typing: Payload is raw dict or None (database/cli.py:231); repository contract is dict[str, object] | None (database/repositories/event_repository.py:30).
  • CLI: Commands are implemented (create/connect/migrate/seed/browse/emit-event) with explicit error exits.
  • Process control API: No start/stop/pause/resume lifecycle API in database module.
  • Key gaps: No structured JSON payload input for emit-event; no typed payload validation at repository boundary.

docs.title: F4 — data-fetcher
docs.text:
• Events: Emits DATA_FETCH_DONE and streaming DATA_FETCH_PROGRESS (modules/data-fetcher/src/rainbow/data_fetcher/data_fetcher.py:494, :790).
  • Payload typing: Raw dict payloads (data_fetcher.py:450, :770, :792).
  • CLI: health-check, fetch, fetch-symbol, fetch-earliest-data, stream, start-vpn implemented with error handling.
  • Process control API: Exposes stream APIs (data_fetcher.py:795) but no explicit lifecycle control methods.
  • Key gaps: No DATA_FETCH_STARTED emission despite STARTED/PROGRESS/DONE expectations in module docs.

docs.title: F5 — data-processing
docs.text:
• Events: Emits DATA_FETCH_DONE in three processing flows (modules/data-processing/src/rainbow/data_processing/data_processing.py:881, :1653, :1852).
  • Payload typing: Raw dict payloads (data_processing.py:809, :1578, :1792).
  • CLI: Listing/health commands exist in modules/data-processing/src/rainbow/data_processing/cli.py.
  • Process control API: Processing methods exist, but no lifecycle control API.
  • Key gaps: Event taxonomy mismatch (DATA_FETCH_DONE emitted from processing module), and no STARTED/PROGRESS lifecycle events.

docs.title: F6 — labeling-service
docs.text:
• Events: Emits LABELS_DONE in both labeling/sampling flows (modules/labeling-service/src/rainbow/labeling_service/labeling_service.py:384, :574).
  • Payload typing: Raw dict payloads (labeling_service.py:368, :559).
  • CLI: health-check, generate-labels, generate-sampling implemented with non-zero failure exits.
  • Process control API: Task APIs exist; no start/stop/pause/resume lifecycle control.
  • Key gaps: No STARTED/PROGRESS event pair for long-running work.

docs.title: F7 — window-preparer
docs.text:
• Events: Emits FAT_WINDOWS_DONE and SMALL_WINDOWS_PROGRESS (modules/window-preparer/src/rainbow/window_preparer/fat_window_preparer.py:389, modules/window-preparer/src/rainbow/window_preparer/small_window_preparer.py:494, :591).
  • Payload typing: Raw dict payloads (fat_window_preparer.py:376, small_window_preparer.py:498).
  • CLI: health-check, generate-fat-windows, prepare-small-window implemented with validations and failure exits.
  • Process control API: One-shot APIs, no lifecycle controller.
  • Key gaps: Missing symmetry: FAT has DONE only; SMALL emits PROGRESS but no DONE.

docs.title: F8 — model-trainer
docs.text:
• Events: Emits TRIAL_STARTED, TRAINING_PROGRESS, TRIAL_COMPLETED, TRIAL_PRUNED, TRIAL_FAILED, ALL_TRIALS_DONE via helper flow (modules/model-trainer/src/rainbow/model_trainer/helpers/optuna/objective.py:481, :634, :695, :815, modules/model-trainer/src/rainbow/model_trainer/helpers/model/training_processing.py:543, modules/model-trainer/src/rainbow/model_trainer/helpers/optuna/trials.py:945).
  • Payload typing: Raw dict payload contract (trials.py:857) sent through repository (database/repositories/event_repository.py:30).
  • CLI: health-check, train, generate-predictions, lit implemented.
  • Process control API: Training entrypoints exist (modules/model-trainer/src/rainbow/model_trainer/model_trainer.py:131) but no explicit stop/pause/resume/status API.
  • Special checks: No DB stop_requested flag in DB schema (modules/database/src/rainbow/database/models.py); no status command in trainer CLI.

docs.title: F9 — backtesting-engine
docs.text:
• Events: Emits BACKTEST_TRIAL_DONE (modules/backtesting-engine/src/rainbow/backtesting_engine/helpers/engine_loop.py:1312).
  • Payload typing: Raw dict payload (engine_loop.py:1304).
  • CLI: run/status/pause/cancel/calibrate implemented with explicit failure handling (modules/backtesting-engine/src/rainbow/backtesting_engine/cli.py).
  • Process control API: pause/cancel/resume-by-session-id exist in Python API (modules/backtesting-engine/src/rainbow/backtesting_engine/backtesting_engine.py:879, :1431, :1532).
  • Key gaps: Missing BACKTEST_STARTED and BACKTEST_PROGRESS emissions versus module event map expectations (modules/common/src/rainbow/common/types/events.py:353).

docs.title: F10 — bot-logic
docs.text:
• Events: No emit call sites found in bot-logic.
  • CLI: Only health-check exists (modules/bot-logic/src/rainbow/bot_logic/cli.py:55).
  • Process control API: Decision APIs exist (bot_logic.py:139, :443), but no lifecycle controls.
  • Key gaps: No event emission surface and very limited CLI observability/control.

docs.title: F11 — live-trading-runner
docs.text:
• Events: Emits LIVE_TRADING_STARTED, LIVE_TRADING_STOPPED, LIVE_ANOMALY_DETECTED, LIVE_EXEC_SUMMARY (modules/live-trading-runner/src/rainbow/live_trading_runner/live_trading_runner.py:241, :274, :283, modules/live-trading-runner/src/rainbow/live_trading_runner/helpers/instance_manager.py:356, :572, :698).
  • Payload typing: Raw dict payloads sent via repository interface (database/repositories/event_repository.py:27).
  • CLI/API parity: CLI and API both expose start/stop/pause/resume/reload/force-close/status/positions (modules/live-trading-runner/src/rainbow/live_trading_runner/cli.py:71, modules/live-trading-runner/src/rainbow/live_trading_runner/live_trading_runner.py:207).
  • Safety checks: kill_switch_enabled and auto_pause_on_data_gap are enforced (runtime_loop.py:527, :551), and anomaly escalation is emitted (instance_manager.py:356).
  • Key gap: heartbeat_timeout_seconds exists in config (modules/common/src/rainbow/common/types/configs/execution_plan_config.py:51) but no runtime enforcement path was found.

docs.title: F12 — housekeeping
docs.text:
• Events: Emits HOUSEKEEPING_DONE (modules/housekeeping/src/rainbow/housekeeping/housekeeping.py:99).
  • Payload typing: Raw payload (housekeeping.py:96).
  • CLI: Large command surface exists in modules/housekeeping/src/rainbow/housekeeping/cli.py.
  • Process control API: Task-oriented APIs only.
  • Key gaps: Several critical methods still marked pending (no operational implementation): run_all (housekeeping.py:79), cleanup_cache (:103), rotate_logs (:115), vacuum_db (:127), report (:1430).

docs.title: F13 — control-panel
docs.text:
• Events: No producer emit_event call sites in control-panel source.
  • CLI: Commands implemented (health-check, HITL and serve flows) in modules/control-panel/src/rainbow/control_panel/cli.py:98.
  • Process control API: Backtest/training async control endpoints/services exist (modules/control-panel/src/rainbow/control_panel/services/backtest_service.py:886, modules/control-panel/src/rainbow/control_panel/services/training_service.py:526).
  • Backend API: REST and WS surfaces are implemented in backend app/routers (modules/control-panel/src/rainbow/control_panel/backend/app.py:102, modules/control-panel/src/rainbow/control_panel/backend/dashboard/extension.py:271).
  • Key gap: Event consumption bridge is not implemented; ControlPanel.get_events() is a dummy placeholder returning empty result (modules/control-panel/src/rainbow/control_panel/control_panel.py:79).

docs.title: F14 — EventConsumer and Event Bridge
docs.text:
• Event consumer runtime: No active EventConsumer/event_bridge implementation found in current modules/ tree.
  • DB support exists: Repository exposes unprocessed polling and acknowledge operations (modules/database/src/rainbow/database/repositories/event_repository.py:52, :76).
  • No production consumer usage: get_unprocessed_events() usage appears only in tests/docs references, not module runtime.
  • Documentation mismatch: docs/EVENTS.md describes thread_id and processed_at fields in event structure (docs/EVENTS.md:115, :119), and docs/DATABASE-SCHEMA.md declares the same (docs/DATABASE-SCHEMA.md:136, :139), but ORM Event model currently has neither field (modules/database/src/rainbow/database/models.py:114-130).
  • Key gap: Supervisor event ingestion has no executable pipeline in current codebase.

docs.title: F15 — emit defaults and event patterns
docs.text:
• Repository default: emit_event has no emit switch; it always writes when called (modules/database/src/rainbow/database/repositories/event_repository.py:27).
  • Module defaults: Most producer APIs default to emit: bool = False, for example data-fetcher, labeling-service, model-trainer, backtesting-engine, window-preparer, and core experiment services (modules/data-fetcher/src/rainbow/data_fetcher/data_fetcher.py:244, modules/labeling-service/src/rainbow/labeling_service/labeling_service.py:101, modules/model-trainer/src/rainbow/model_trainer/model_trainer.py:135, modules/backtesting-engine/src/rainbow/backtesting_engine/backtesting_engine.py:872, modules/window-preparer/src/rainbow/window_preparer/fat_window_preparer.py:131, modules/core/src/rainbow/core/services/experiment_data_service.py:356).
  • Pattern outcome: Event emission is opt-in and frequently disabled by default at module API layer.
  • Storage reality: Events are persisted in DB with event_uuid, event_type, payload, processed, experiment_id, trial_id (modules/database/src/rainbow/database/models.py:119-127), but no thread_id/processed_at columns in current ORM model.

docs.title: F16 — Event declaration vs implementation gap analysis
docs.text:
• Declared: EventType enum currently defines 77 events in modules/common/src/rainbow/common/types/events.py.
  • Emitted (hardcoded active code paths): 19 event types are emitted in active module code: ALL_TRIALS_DONE, BACKTEST_TRIAL_DONE, DATA_FETCH_DONE, DATA_FETCH_PROGRESS, FAT_WINDOWS_DONE, HOUSEKEEPING_DONE, LABELS_DONE, LIVE_ANOMALY_DETECTED, LIVE_EXEC_SUMMARY, LIVE_TRADING_STARTED, LIVE_TRADING_STOPPED, MIGRATION_APPLIED, MIGRATION_GENERATED, SMALL_WINDOWS_PROGRESS, TRAINING_PROGRESS, TRIAL_COMPLETED, TRIAL_FAILED, TRIAL_PRUNED, TRIAL_STARTED.
  • Gap 1 (declared not emitted): 58 enum events were not found in active emit call paths (notably many REQUESTED/STARTED orchestration events, several closure/summary events, and store/budget/thread observability events).
  • Gap 2 (emitted not declared): No hardcoded emitted events outside EventType were found.
  • Gap 3 (docs not enum):
  • docs/EVENTS.md still includes deprecated aliases: DATA_FETCH_COMPLETE, LABELING_COMPLETE, BACKTEST_COMPLETE, TRAINING_COMPLETE (docs/EVENTS.md:99-102).
  • docs/MASTER-PLAN.md references TRIAL_SUMMARY_READY, which is not an enum value.
  • Gap 4 (enum not docs): Large subset of enum events (notably HITL/BUDGET/STORE/SYSTEM_TASK/GRAPH_THREAD groups) are under-documented in docs/EVENTS.md.
  • Decision required for Stage 2: Normalize canonical event set, then derive execution plan tasks for removal/implementation/documentation alignment.

docs.title: Event System — DB Schema & Traceability Design
docs.text: Status: DRAFT v3 Date: 2026-03-24 Depends on: EVENT_TAXONOMY_SIMPLE_VIEW.md (canonical event list), EVENT_TAXONOMY_FINAL_PAYLOADS.md (payload contracts) Scope: Database schema changes, naming conventions, traceability architecture, and Task lifecycle design for the event system refactor

docs.title: Suffixes
docs.text:
Every field name in the system follows these suffix rules:

Suffix Meaning Example Points to _id Integer FK to a DB table PK experiment_id, task_id, trial_id table.id column _uuid UUID string, self-owned by this record task_uuid, event_uuid, execution_plan_uuid No FK — generated at creation, stable across restarts _ref String referencing an external system (NOT a DB table) thread_ref External routing key (e.g., LangGraph thread) _hash Deterministic hash of a configuration label_config_hash, fat_hash, cache_key Content-addressable — same config = same hash

docs.title: Rule
docs.text: If you see _id → there MUST be a table with that PK. If there's no table → it's NOT _id.

docs.title: Why thread_ref and not thread_id
docs.text: thread_ref references a LangGraph conversation thread. There is no threads table in our DB. LangGraph manages its own thread state via its checkpointer (SQLite/Postgres). Our system only stores the thread reference string for routing — "when this task completes, resume THIS thread."

docs.title: Table: tasks
docs.text:
tasks
├── id                          INT PK AUTO
├── task_uuid                   VARCHAR(36) UNIQUE INDEXED — stable UUID
├── task_type                   VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL — TaskType enum
├── thread_id                   VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL INDEXED — ⚠️ TO RENAME → thread_ref
├── experiment_id               INT FK → experiments.id (nullable)
├── trial_id                    INT FK → trials.id (nullable)
├── status                      VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT "pending" INDEXED — TaskStatus enum
├── expected_duration_seconds   FLOAT NOT NULL DEFAULT 60.0
├── started_at                  DATETIME(tz) NOT NULL
├── completed_at                DATETIME(tz) nullable
├── created_at                  DATETIME(tz) server default
└── updated_at                  DATETIME(tz) server default

Relationships: - events → 1:N with events.task_id - experiment → N:1 with experiments.id - trial → N:1 with trials.id - parquets → 1:N with parquets.task_id

docs.title: Table: events
docs.text:
events
├── id              INT PK AUTO
├── event_uuid      VARCHAR(36) UNIQUE INDEXED — stable UUID
├── event_type      ENUM(EventType) NOT NULL
├── payload         JSON nullable — event-specific data
├── processed       BOOL NOT NULL DEFAULT false — Event Bridge consumption flag
├── task_id         INT FK → tasks.id (nullable)
├── experiment_id   INT FK → experiments.id (nullable)
├── trial_id        INT FK → trials.id (nullable)
├── created_at      DATETIME(tz) server default
└── updated_at      DATETIME(tz) server default

docs.text:
class TaskType(StrEnum):
    DATA_FETCH = "data_fetch"
    DATA_PROCESS = "data_process"
