docs.title: What exists and what's missing
docs.text: Component Exists? Notes tasks table ✅ Model defined in models.py events table ✅ Model defined in models.py EventRepository ✅ Full CRUD + emit_event(), get_unprocessed_events(), mark_event_processed() TaskRepository ❌ Referenced in MCP memory but file does not exist parent_task_id in tasks ❌ No hierarchy support between orchestrated Tasks thread_ref naming ❌ Currently named thread_id (misleading) TaskType.LIVE_TRADING ❌ Missing from enum TaskType.DATA_PROCESSING ⚠️ Named DATA_PROCESS — inconsistent with event family name DATA_PROCESSING_*

docs.title: 3.1 Table tasks — Modifications
docs.text:
tasks
  ├── id                          INT PK AUTO
  ├── task_uuid                   VARCHAR(36) UNIQUE INDEXED
  ├── task_type                   VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL — TaskType enum
- ├── thread_id                   VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL INDEXED
+ ├── thread_ref                  VARCHAR(255) nullable INDEXED
  ├── experiment_id               INT FK → experiments.id (nullable)
  ├── trial_id                    INT FK → trials.id (nullable)
+ ├── parent_task_id              INT FK → tasks.id (nullable) INDEXED
  ├── status                      VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT "pending" INDEXED
  ├── expected_duration_seconds   FLOAT NOT NULL DEFAULT 60.0
  ├── started_at                  DATETIME(tz) NOT NULL
  ├── completed_at                DATETIME(tz) nullable
  ├── created_at                  DATETIME(tz) server default
  └── updated_at                  DATETIME(tz) server default

Changes explained:

Change Why thread_id → thread_ref Not a FK — it's an external reference to a LangGraph thread. Follows naming convention. thread_ref nullable (was NOT NULL) Tasks launched from CLI or standalone scripts have no graph thread. Only orchestrator-spawned tasks have a thread_ref. + parent_task_id FK → tasks.id Self-referencing FK for chaining orchestrated Tasks. E.g., LABELING task spawned as part of a pipeline that started with DATA_FETCH.

New relationships on Task model:

# Self-referencing hierarchy for orchestrated task chains
parent_task_id: Mapped[int | None] = mapped_column(ForeignKey("tasks.id"), nullable=True, index=True)
parent_task: Mapped[Task | None] = relationship("Task", remote_side=[id], back_populates="child_tasks")
child_tasks: Mapped[list[Task]] = relationship("Task", back_populates="parent_task")

Important: parent_task_id links orchestrated Tasks only (the 8 TaskTypes). Phase/Trial are NOT Tasks — their hierarchy is tracked via domain IDs in payloads (§4.2).

docs.title: 3.2 Table events — No Column Changes
docs.text:
The events table does NOT change. Rationale:

  • thread_ref is NOT duplicated here. To find the thread for an event: event.task → task.thread_ref. One JOIN, trivial at our volume.
  • task_uuid and parent_task_uuid live in the JSON payload (via BaseEventPayload), not as DB columns. The structured column task_id (FK) already provides the DB-level link.
  • No denormalization. Schema stays normalized.

docs.title: 3.3 Enum TaskType — Additions
docs.text:
class TaskType(StrEnum):
      DATA_FETCH = "data_fetch"
-     DATA_PROCESS = "data_process"
+     DATA_PROCESSING = "data_processing"
      LABELING = "labeling"
      FAT_WINDOWS = "fat_windows"
      SMALL_WINDOWS = "small_windows"
      TRAINING = "training"
      BACKTEST = "backtest"
+     LIVE_TRADING = "live_trading"

Changes:

Change Why DATA_PROCESS → DATA_PROCESSING Consistent with event family name DATA_PROCESSING_* + LIVE_TRADING Live trading sessions are orchestrator-dispatched work units

Why NO PHASE, TRIAL, or HOUSEKEEPING?

A Task represents work dispatched by the orchestrator (or CLI) that the Event Bridge needs to track for routing/timeout. Phases and Trials are internal steps of the model-trainer module — nobody dispatches them, nobody awaits them via Event Bridge. Their events exist for observability, not for routing. The hierarchy Training → Phase → Trial → Epoch is expressed through domain IDs in payloads (study_id, phase_number, trial_id, trial_number, epoch), not through Task records.

docs.title: 3.4 BaseEventPayload — Traceability Fields
docs.text:
class BaseEventPayload(BaseModel):
    """Base class for all event payloads."""

    # Traceability — links this event to its task lifecycle
    task_uuid: str | None = Field(default=None, description="UUID of the task this event belongs to")
    parent_task_uuid: str | None = Field(default=None, description="UUID of the parent task in the pipeline chain")

    # Existing fields
    source: str | None = Field(default=None, description="Module that emitted the event")
    note: str | None = Field(default=None, description="Optional debug note")

Why UUIDs in payload (not IDs)?

  • The payload JSON travels outside the DB context — into LangGraph agent state, into MCP messages, into logs, into webhooks.
  • Integer IDs are DB-internal. UUIDs are globally meaningful.
  • The DB-level FK (events.task_id → tasks.id) handles SQL queries. The payload UUID handles everything else.

What task_uuid and parent_task_uuid answer:

Field Question it answers Who uses it task_uuid "This event belongs to which task lifecycle?" Event Bridge (resume the right thread), Agents (group events), Dashboard (show lifecycle) parent_task_uuid "This task was spawned by which bigger task?" Agents (understand pipeline chain), Dashboard (drill-down), Debugging (trace root cause up)

Note: parent_task_uuid links orchestrated Tasks (the 8 TaskTypes). Phase/Trial events do NOT use parent_task_uuid — they all share the TRAINING task's task_uuid and express hierarchy via domain IDs (§4.2).

docs.title: 4.1 How Tasks and Events Relate
docs.text:
Task (DB record)                           Events (DB records)
┌──────────────────────┐                   ┌──────────────────────┐
│ task_uuid: "abc-123" │                   │ LABELS_REQUESTED     │
│ task_type: LABELING  │◄──────────────────│   task_id: 42        │
│ thread_ref: "exp-1"  │                   │   payload.task_uuid: │
│ parent_task_id: 40   │                   │     "abc-123"        │
│ status: running      │                   │   payload.parent_    │
│ experiment_id: 5     │                   │     task_uuid: "p-1" │
│                      │                   ├──────────────────────┤
│                      │                   │ LABELS_STARTED       │
│                      │◄──────────────────│   task_id: 42        │
│                      │                   ├──────────────────────┤
│                      │                   │ LABELS_PROGRESS      │
│                      │◄──────────────────│   task_id: 42        │
│                      │                   ├──────────────────────┤
│                      │                   │ LABELS_DONE          │
│                      │◄──────────────────│   task_id: 42        │
└──────────────────────┘                   └──────────────────────┘

One Task → N Events (all sharing the same task_id FK)
All 4 events share task_uuid="abc-123" in their payload

docs.title: 4.2 Training Task — All Events Under One Task
docs.text:
Phase, Trial, and Epoch are internal steps of the model-trainer module, not separate Tasks. All their events point to the same TRAINING Task via task_id. The internal hierarchy is expressed through domain IDs in payloads.

Task: TRAINING (task_uuid="t-001", task_type=TRAINING, thread_ref="exp-main")
  │
  │  All events below share: task_id=42, payload.task_uuid="t-001"
  │  Internal hierarchy via domain IDs: study_id, phase_number, trial_id, trial_number, epoch
  │
  ├── Event: TRAINING_REQUESTED   experiment_id=5
  ├── Event: TRAINING_STARTED     experiment_id=5, total_phases=3, total_trials_planned=27
  │
  ├── Event: PHASE_STARTED        experiment_id=5, study_id=10, phase_number=1, total_phases=3, planned_trials=10, data_fraction=0.3
  │   ├── Event: TRIAL_STARTED    experiment_id=5, study_id=10, trial_id=101, trial_number=0, phase_number=1
  │   ├── Event: TRIAL_PROGRESS   experiment_id=5, study_id=10, trial_id=101, epoch=1, total_epochs=50, train_loss=0.8, ...
  │   ├── Event: TRIAL_PROGRESS   experiment_id=5, study_id=10, trial_id=101, epoch=2, total_epochs=50, train_loss=0.6, ...
  │   ├── Event: TRIAL_DONE       experiment_id=5, study_id=10, trial_id=101, outcome="completed", score=0.75
  │   ├── Event: TRIAL_STARTED    experiment_id=5, study_id=10, trial_id=102, trial_number=1, phase_number=1
  │   ├── Event: TRIAL_DONE       experiment_id=5, study_id=10, trial_id=102, outcome="pruned", step_pruned_at=5
  │   └── Event: PHASE_PROGRESS   experiment_id=5, study_id=10, phase_number=1, trials_completed=2, planned_trials=10
  ├── Event: PHASE_DONE           experiment_id=5, study_id=10, phase_number=1, n_completed=8, n_pruned=2
  │
  ├── Event: TRAINING_PROGRESS    experiment_id=5, phases_completed=1, total_phases=3, trials_completed=10
  │
  ├── Event: PHASE_STARTED        experiment_id=5, study_id=11, phase_number=2, ...
  │   └── ...                     (same pattern: trials, epochs, progress)
  ├── Event: PHASE_DONE           experiment_id=5, study_id=11, phase_number=2, ...
  │
  ├── Event: TRAINING_PROGRESS    experiment_id=5, phases_completed=2, total_phases=3, trials_completed=20
  │
  └── Event: TRAINING_DONE        experiment_id=5, phases_completed=3, total_phases=3, status="completed", best_trial_id=101

How to query the Phase/Trial hierarchy (via domain IDs in payloads, not parent_task_id):

Question Query All events for this Training WHERE task_id = 42 All events for Phase 1 WHERE task_id = 42 AND payload->>'phase_number' = '1' All events for Trial 101 WHERE task_id = 42 AND payload->>'trial_id' = '101' (or WHERE trial_id = 101 via DB FK) Phases completed so far Last TRAINING_PROGRESS event → phases_completed field Trials completed in Phase 1 Last PHASE_PROGRESS with phase_number=1 → trials_completed field

docs.title: 4.3 Event Bridge Flow
docs.text:
Orchestrator (LangGraph)              Module                    Event Bridge
│                                     │                         │
│  1. Create Task (status=pending)    │                         │
│     task_uuid="abc-123"             │                         │
│     thread_ref="exp-main"           │                         │
│                                     │                         │
│  2. Emit LABELS_REQUESTED           │                         │
│     task_id=42                      │                         │
│     payload.task_uuid="abc-123"     │                         │
│                                     │                         │
│  3. graph.interrupt()  ─────────────┼───────────────────────→ │
│     (waiting for resume)            │                         │
│                                     │                         │
│                                     │  4. Pick up task        │
│                                     │     Update status=running│
│                                     │                         │
│                                     │  5. Emit LABELS_STARTED │
│                                     │     task_id=42          │
│                                     │                         │
│                                     │  6. Do work...          │
│                                     │     Emit LABELS_PROGRESS│
│                                     │                         │
│                                     │  7. Emit LABELS_DONE    │
│                                     │     task_id=42          │
│                                     │     Update task         │
│                                     │     status=completed    │
│                                     │                         │
│                                     │                         │  8. Poll: get_unprocessed_events()
│                                     │                         │     Finds LABELS_DONE
│                                     │                         │     event.task → task.thread_ref
│                                     │                         │     → "exp-main"
│                                     │                         │
│  9. graph.invoke(                   │                         │
│       Command(resume=payload),      │                  ←──────│
│       config=thread_ref             │                         │
│     )                               │                         │
│                                     │                         │  10. mark_event_processed()
│  10. Director wakes up              │                         │
│      Reads payload                  │                         │
│      Decides next action            │                         │
│                                     │                         │

Key point: The Event Bridge resolves thread_ref by doing event.task_id → task.thread_ref. One JOIN. No denormalization needed.

docs.title: 5. Payload 4-Layer Pattern
docs.text: Every event payload follows this layered structure:

docs.title: Layer 1: Identity (ALL events in the family)
docs.text:
Fields that route and identify the work unit. Present in REQUESTED, STARTED, PROGRESS, and DONE.

task_uuid, parent_task_uuid          ← from BaseEventPayload
experiment_id, study_id?, trial_id   ← domain-specific routing IDs

docs.title: Layer 2: Config Fingerprint (STARTED + DONE)
docs.text:
Deterministic hash of the configuration that defines what this task does. For families where the config matters for caching or change detection.

label_config_hash    ← Labels family
fat_hash             ← FAT Windows family
cache_key            ← SMALL Windows family
pipeline_hash?       ← Data Processing (if implemented)

Families that DON'T need config hashes:

Family Why no hash Data Fetch Identified by symbol + timeframe + range — hash would be redundant Training Identified by experiment_id + training config is immutable per experiment Phase Identified by study_id + phase_number Trial Identified by trial_id (DB PK) Backtest Identified by backtest_session_id (DB PK) Live Trading Identified by execution_plan_id + live_session_id

docs.title: Layer 3: Key Parameters (STARTED)
docs.text:
Human-readable parameters that define the behavior of this task. Not for machines (that's what the hash is for) — for humans reading logs, dashboards, and agent summaries.

threshold_type, horizon              ← Labels: "what kind of labeling run is this?"
window_size, features_count          ← FAT Windows: "how big is this FAT build?"
data_fraction, feature_subset_size   ← SMALL Windows: "how much data, how many features?"
total_phases, total_trials_planned   ← Training: "how big is this training run?"

docs.title: Layer 4: Variable Data (PROGRESS + DONE)
docs.text:
Data that changes during execution or represents the final outcome.

PROGRESS: position counters + live metrics

processed_rows, total_rows, progress_percent    ← where am I?
train_loss, val_loss, f1_macro                  ← how is it going? (Trial)

DONE: outcome + key results

status/outcome, duration_seconds                ← how did it end?
count_long, count_short, count_none             ← what was produced? (Labels)
total_pnl, return_pct, sharpe_ratio             ← what was the result? (Backtest)

docs.title: Layer Summary
docs.text:
Layer What REQUESTED STARTED PROGRESS DONE 1. Identity IDs, routing ✅ ✅ ✅ ✅ 2. Config hash Fingerprint ❌ ✅ ❌ ✅ 3. Key params Human-readable config ❌ ✅ ❌ ❌ 4. Variable data Progress/results ❌ ❌ ✅ ✅

REQUESTED is special: It carries Layer 1 (identity) + the parameters the caller passes to trigger the work. It does NOT carry config hashes (not computed yet) or key params (not resolved yet). But it MUST be self-contained — enough data for the Event Bridge to trigger the module.

docs.title: 6. TaskRepository — Required Methods
docs.text:
New file: modules/database/src/rainbow/database/repositories/task_repository.py

class TaskRepository(BaseRepository):
    """Repository for Task lifecycle operations."""

    def create_task(
        self,
        task_type: TaskType,
        *,
        thread_ref: str | None = None,
        experiment_id: int | None = None,
        trial_id: int | None = None,
        parent_task_id: int | None = None,
        expected_duration_seconds: float = 60.0,
    ) -> Task:
        """Create a new task with status=pending and generated task_uuid."""

    def get_by_uuid(self, task_uuid: str) -> Task | None:
        """Find a task by its UUID."""

    def get_by_id(self, task_id: int) -> Task | None:
        """Find a task by its PK."""

    def update_status(
        self,
        task_id: int,
        status: TaskStatus,
        *,
        completed_at: datetime | None = None,
    ) -> Task | None:
        """Update task status. Sets completed_at when transitioning to terminal states."""

    def get_children(self, task_id: int) -> list[Task]:
        """Get all child tasks of a parent task (one level down)."""

    def find_by_thread_ref(self, thread_ref: str) -> list[Task]:
        """Find all tasks associated with a graph thread."""

    def find_timed_out(self) -> list[Task]:
        """Find running tasks that exceeded their expected_duration_seconds."""

    def get_lineage(self, task_id: int) -> list[Task]:
        """Walk parent_task_id chain upward. Returns [self, parent, grandparent, ...]."""

docs.title: 7. emit_event() — Updated Signature
docs.text:
The existing EventRepository.emit_event() already accepts task_id as a parameter (it's used to set the FK column). No signature change needed for the DB-level link.

However, the caller code needs to:

  1. Create the Task FIRST (via TaskRepository.create_task())
  2. Pass task_id to emit_event() when emitting REQUESTED/STARTED/PROGRESS/DONE
  3. Include task_uuid and parent_task_uuid in the payload dict

Example flow in module code:

# 1. Orchestrator creates the Task (chained from a previous DATA_FETCH task)
task = task_repo.create_task(
    task_type=TaskType.LABELING,
    thread_ref="exp-main-thread",
    experiment_id=experiment_id,
    parent_task_id=previous_task.id,  # chain to parent task in the pipeline
)

# 2. Emit REQUESTED with task linkage
event_repo.emit_event(
    event_type=EventType.LABELS_REQUESTED,
    payload={
        "task_uuid": task.task_uuid,
        "parent_task_uuid": previous_task.task_uuid,
        "experiment_id": experiment_id,
    },
    experiment_id=experiment_id,
    task_id=task.id,
)

# 3. Module picks up, emits STARTED
event_repo.emit_event(
    event_type=EventType.LABELS_STARTED,
    payload={
        "task_uuid": task.task_uuid,
        "parent_task_uuid": previous_task.task_uuid,
        "experiment_id": experiment_id,
        "label_config_hash": config_hash,
        "threshold_type": "atr_based",
        "horizon": 24,
    },
    experiment_id=experiment_id,
    task_id=task.id,
)

docs.title: DB Schema Changes (Alembic Migration)
docs.text: # Table Change SQL 1 tasks Rename column thread_id → thread_ref ALTER TABLE tasks RENAME COLUMN thread_id TO thread_ref 2 tasks Make thread_ref nullable ALTER TABLE tasks ALTER COLUMN thread_ref DROP NOT NULL 3 tasks Add parent_task_id column ALTER TABLE tasks ADD COLUMN parent_task_id INTEGER REFERENCES tasks(id) 4 tasks Add index on parent_task_id CREATE INDEX ix_tasks_parent_task_id ON tasks(parent_task_id)

docs.title: Python Model Changes
docs.text: # File Change 1 models.py — Task Rename thread_id → thread_ref, make nullable 2 models.py — Task Add parent_task_id FK + parent_task/child_tasks relationships 3 enums.py — TaskType Rename DATA_PROCESS → DATA_PROCESSING, add LIVE_TRADING 4 events.py — BaseEventPayload Add task_uuid, parent_task_uuid fields

docs.title: New Files
docs.text: # File Content 1 repositories/task_repository.py TaskRepository with full CRUD + lifecycle methods 2 migrations/versions/xxxx_add_task_hierarchy.py Alembic migration for schema changes

docs.title: Code Updates (All Modules)
docs.text: # Scope Change 1 Every emit_event() callsite Pass task_id parameter + include task_uuid/parent_task_uuid in payload 2 Every module entry point Create Task record before starting work 3 Every module completion Update Task status to completed/failed 4 EventRepository.emit_event() No signature change — already supports task_id 5 All references to Task.thread_id Rename to Task.thread_ref

docs.title: 9. What Does NOT Change
docs.text: Item Reason events table columns No new columns. task_id FK already exists. events.event_uuid Already exists, keeps working as unique event identifier events.processed flag Already exists, Event Bridge pattern unchanged experiments table No changes needed trials table No changes needed backtest_sessions table No changes needed execution_plans table No changes needed live_sessions table No changes needed

docs.title: "Show me all events for a specific task lifecycle"
docs.text:
SELECT e.* FROM events e
WHERE e.task_id = (SELECT id FROM tasks WHERE task_uuid = 'abc-123')
ORDER BY e.created_at;

docs.title: "Find the thread to resume for this DONE event"
docs.text:
SELECT t.thread_ref
FROM events e
JOIN tasks t ON e.task_id = t.id
WHERE e.id = :event_id;

docs.title: "Get all events for a Training run (including Phase/Trial/Epoch events)"
docs.text:
-- All events under the TRAINING task — includes Phase, Trial, Epoch events
SELECT e.* FROM events e
WHERE e.task_id = (SELECT id FROM tasks WHERE task_uuid = 't-001')
ORDER BY e.created_at;

docs.title: "Get all Trial events for Phase 1"
docs.text:
-- Filter by domain IDs in payload (Phase/Trial hierarchy is in the payload, not in Task records)
SELECT e.* FROM events e
WHERE e.task_id = 42
  AND e.event_type IN ('TRIAL_STARTED', 'TRIAL_PROGRESS', 'TRIAL_DONE')
  AND json_extract(e.payload, '$.phase_number') = 1
ORDER BY e.created_at;

docs.title: "Trace a Task up its pipeline chain"
docs.text:
-- Walk parent_task_id chain: LABELING → DATA_PROCESSING → DATA_FETCH
WITH RECURSIVE lineage AS (
    SELECT * FROM tasks WHERE task_uuid = 'abc-123'
    UNION ALL
    SELECT parent.* FROM tasks parent
    JOIN lineage child ON child.parent_task_id = parent.id
)
SELECT * FROM lineage;

docs.title: "Find timed-out tasks"
docs.text:
SELECT * FROM tasks
WHERE status = 'running'
AND (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP - started_at) > (expected_duration_seconds || ' seconds')::interval;

docs.title: 11. Open Questions
docs.text:
1. Task creation responsibility: Who creates the Task record — the orchestrator (before emitting REQUESTED) or the module (upon receiving the work)? Proposal: orchestrator creates it because only the orchestrator knows the thread_ref.

  2. CLI/standalone tasks: When a module runs from CLI (no orchestrator), should it still create a Task record? Proposal: yes, with thread_ref=None. This ensures ALL work is traceable, not just orchestrated work.

  3. HITL tasks: HITL governance gates are not really "tasks" in the compute sense. Should they create Task records? Proposal: no — HITL events use their own action field as the primary key, not task_uuid.

  4. SMALL_WINDOWS tasks: SMALL windows are called internally per-trial by model-trainer, not dispatched by the orchestrator. Should they create Task records? If so, they'd use thread_ref=None. Or should SMALL_WINDOWS events just share the TRAINING Task's task_id? This affects whether SMALL_WINDOWS stays in TaskType.

  5. Retry semantics: When a task fails and is retried, should the retry be a NEW task with parent_task_id pointing to the failed task, or a NEW task with the SAME parent_task_id? Proposal: same parent — the retry is a sibling, not a child of the failure.

docs.title: Event Taxonomy — Final Payloads
docs.text: Status: DRAFT v3 — Process-grounded rewrite Generated: 2026-03-24 Source of truth for event names: EVENT_TAXONOMY_SIMPLE_VIEW.md Source of truth for payloads: This document (once approved) Grounded in: Actual code business logic of every module (function signatures, configs, data flows)

docs.title: Format Convention
docs.text:
• Fields marked with ? are optional. All others are required.
  • IDs always go first in every payload, even when optional for that event.
  • IDs have the same optionality across the entire family — if experiment_id is optional in one event, it's optional in all.
  • Every payload inherits BaseEventPayload (provides source?, note? — not listed below).
  • Rules applied to one event in a family apply to all events in that family.

docs.title: Payload Design Rules
docs.text:
1. Identifiers first — experiment_id, then study_id / trial_id / backtest_session_id / etc.
  2. Required = the emitter ALWAYS has this value at emission time. Not "should have" — HAS.
  3. Optional = the emitter might not have this value depending on the invocation path.
  4. No duplicate sources of truth — if it's in the experiment config, don't repeat it. Only the ID + what changed.
  5. REQUESTED = what the caller passes — resolved parameters known at request time.
  6. STARTED = REQUESTED + newly created IDs — session IDs, validated/aligned ranges.
  7. PROGRESS = IDs + position in the work — where am I, how far along.
  8. DONE = IDs + outcome — what happened, key results. Heavy data goes to DB.
  9. Consistent within a family — same fields, same optionality, same naming.

docs.title: A. Data Fetch
docs.text:
Process: Downloads historical OHLCV candles for ONE symbol+timeframe combination. Can be invoked from experiment (orchestrator) or standalone (CLI). Provider is ALWAYS resolved before any work starts — either from ExperimentConfig.data_provider or from active DataProviderConfig.

DATA_FETCH_REQUESTED:  experiment_id?, symbol, timeframe, provider, range_start, range_end
DATA_FETCH_STARTED:    experiment_id?, symbol, timeframe, provider, range_start, range_end
DATA_FETCH_PROGRESS:   experiment_id?, symbol, timeframe, provider, fetched_rows, total_rows, progress_percent
DATA_FETCH_DONE:       experiment_id?, symbol, timeframe, provider, rows_fetched, parquet_id?

Why each field:

  • experiment_id? — Optional across the entire family. When fetch(experiment_id=123) is called, it's present. When fetch_symbol(symbol="BTCUSDT", ...) is called standalone or from CLI, there's no experiment.
  • symbol — Always singular. Code operates on base_symbol (one symbol per fetch call). Never plural.
  • timeframe — Always known at request time. You can't request a fetch without knowing what timeframe you need.
  • provider — Always resolved before any event fires. Either from ExperimentConfig.data_provider (with overrides applied via apply_experiment_data_provider()) or from the active DataProviderConfig. Never unknown.
  • range_start, range_end — Explicit dates. From ExperimentConfig.range.start/end or CLI --start/--end. STARTED may have adjusted/aligned values (last_tick_start logic, provider earliest clipping).
  • parquet_id? — Only present when save_parquet=True and DB registration succeeds.

Changes from v2: symbols (plural) → symbol (singular, matching code reality). Added timeframe to REQUESTED (can't request without it). provider required everywhere (always resolved). time_range → explicit range_start/range_end. Removed stream_mode (streaming is a separate code path, not a mode flag).

docs.title: B. Data Processing
docs.text:
Process: Computes feature DAG for an experiment — transforms raw OHLCV through pipelines (CLEAN_PRICE, RELATIVE_PRICE) and indicators (RSI, EMA, etc.). Each ExtendedFeature node is processed independently. The orchestrator calls this once per experiment; internally it iterates over all feature nodes in dependency order.

DATA_PROCESSING_REQUESTED:  experiment_id
DATA_PROCESSING_STARTED:    experiment_id, features_total
DATA_PROCESSING_PROGRESS:   experiment_id, features_processed, features_total, progress_percent, current_feature?
DATA_PROCESSING_DONE:       experiment_id, features_processed, features_total, features_failed, duration_seconds

Why each field:

  • experiment_id — Always required. Data processing always runs for an experiment (features come from ExperimentRequirements.build_experiment_requirements()).
  • features_total — Count of ExtendedFeature nodes in the dependency tree. Known at start after build_experiment_requirements().
  • features_processed — How many nodes completed successfully so far.
  • features_failed — How many nodes failed (partial processing is possible).
  • current_feature? — Human-readable name of the feature being processed now (e.g., "RSI(14) on BTCUSDT:5m"). Optional because the last PROGRESS before DONE may not have a "current" one.
  • duration_seconds — Total wall time for all features.

Changes from v2: Removed per-feature detail from events (feature_name, symbol, timeframe, feature_hash, pipeline_hash, parquet_path, parquet_id, stage_name, status, rows). That's per-node logging, not event-level. Events track the high-level job: "how many of N features are done?" Individual feature results and validation go to structured logs and DB.

docs.title: C. Labels
docs.text:
Process: Generates LONG/SHORT/NONE labels for each candle based on future price movement over a horizon window. Always runs per experiment. Uses OHLCV + ATR (if ATR-based thresholds). One labeling config per experiment, three label classes, no "label families".

LABELS_REQUESTED:  experiment_id
LABELS_STARTED:    experiment_id, label_config_hash, threshold_type, horizon
LABELS_PROGRESS:   experiment_id, processed_rows, total_rows, progress_percent
LABELS_DONE:       experiment_id, label_config_hash, count_long, count_short, count_none, count_total

Why each field:

  • experiment_id — Always required. generate_labels_for_experiment(experiment_id) is the only entry point.
  • label_config_hash — Computed from LabelingConfig (threshold_type + horizon + thresholds + drawdown limits). Identifies which config version produced these labels. Known after config loads (STARTED), and carried through to DONE for traceability.
  • threshold_type — "percentage" / "atr_based" / "atr_based_absolute". The single most important parameter that changes labeling behavior. Worth surfacing in STARTED so you immediately know what kind of run this is.
  • horizon — Look-ahead candles (e.g., 24). Defines how many future candles the label evaluates. Critical parameter.
  • processed_rows / total_rows — Labeling is row-by-row (vectorized, but progress is per-row). total_rows = len(ohlcv) - horizon (last horizon rows dropped since no future data).
  • count_long, count_short, count_none, count_total — The result. Code today emits these as nested dict — flatten.

Changes from v2: Removed label_config_hash from REQUESTED (caller just passes experiment_id — hash is computed by the service). Added threshold_type and horizon to STARTED (key behavior parameters). Removed label_family? (doesn't exist — no label families). Removed range_start?/range_end? (range comes from experiment config, no need to repeat). Removed warnings?/errors? from DONE (validation details go to logs/DB). Made label_config_hash required in both STARTED and DONE (always computable).

docs.title: D. FAT Windows
docs.text:
Process: Builds flattened feature windows for each sampling timestamp. One row per potential trade entry, containing window_size historical values for every feature. Input: sampling parquet (timestamps + labels) + all feature parquets. Output: one large FAT parquet.

FAT_WINDOWS_REQUESTED:  experiment_id
FAT_WINDOWS_STARTED:    experiment_id, window_size, features_count, sampling_rows
FAT_WINDOWS_PROGRESS:   experiment_id, windows_built, total_windows, progress_percent
FAT_WINDOWS_DONE:       experiment_id, fat_hash, n_windows, n_columns, fat_parquet_id?

Why each field:

  • experiment_id — Always required. generate_fat_windows_for_experiment(experiment_id) is the only entry.
  • window_size — From experiment config. Defines how many historical candles each window contains (e.g., 30). Determines the shape of the output.
  • features_count — Number of features being expanded into windows. Known after build_experiment_requirements().
  • sampling_rows — Total rows in the sampling parquet (= total windows to build). Known at start.
  • windows_built / total_windows — Progress through the sampling timestamps. total_windows = sampling_rows.
  • fat_hash — Deterministic hash of window_size + window_features + output_columns + feature hashes. Identifies this FAT configuration for caching/change detection.
  • n_windows — Final count of windows built (may differ from sampling_rows if some timestamps are invalid).
  • n_columns — Total columns in the FAT parquet (e.g., features_count × window_size + label columns).
  • fat_parquet_id? — DB parquet record ID. Only present when save_parquet=True.

Changes from v2: Added window_size, features_count, sampling_rows to STARTED (the parameters that define the FAT build). Renamed feature_count → features_count (consistent naming). Added n_columns to DONE (critical info — how wide is the FAT?). Moved fat_hash to DONE (it's a result identifier). Removed range_start?/range_end? (from experiment config). Removed warnings?/errors? (logs/DB).

docs.title: E. SMALL Windows
docs.text:
Process: Creates a trial-specific subset of the FAT parquet. Selects specific feature columns (from Optuna suggestion) and optionally reduces rows (via data_fraction). Can be reused across trials if the cache_key matches. Called per-trial from the training loop.

SMALL_WINDOWS_REQUESTED:  experiment_id, trial_id
SMALL_WINDOWS_STARTED:    experiment_id, trial_id, cache_key, feature_subset_size, data_fraction, reused
SMALL_WINDOWS_PROGRESS:   experiment_id, trial_id, cache_key, columns_processed, total_columns, progress_percent
SMALL_WINDOWS_DONE:       experiment_id, trial_id, cache_key, reused, small_parquet_id?

Why each field:

  • experiment_id, trial_id — Always required. prepare_small_window(experiment_id, trial_id, ...) takes both.
  • cache_key — Hash of fat_parquet_id + window_size + feature_columns_with_weights. If a SMALL with this key exists with enough rows → cache hit. Known after parameter resolution.
  • feature_subset_size — How many feature columns were selected for this trial (e.g., 15 out of 60). From Optuna's feature_columns_with_weights suggestion.
  • data_fraction — From PhaseConfig.data_fraction (e.g., 0.3 = use 30% of rows). Defines how much of the FAT to keep.
  • reused — True if cache hit (existing SMALL reused), False if built from scratch. Known at STARTED because the cache check happens before any work.
  • columns_processed / total_columns — If building from scratch, tracks column selection progress. If reused=True, PROGRESS may not fire at all (skip straight to DONE).
  • small_parquet_id? — DB record ID. Only if registered in DB.

Changes from v2: Added data_fraction to STARTED (the key parameter — what fraction of data this trial sees). Made feature_subset_size required in STARTED (always known from Optuna suggestion). Made reused required in both STARTED and DONE (always known — cache check happens before STARTED fires).

docs.title: F. Training (container level)
docs.text:
Process: Top-level orchestration of multi-phase progressive training. Resolves all phases from config, creates the OptunaStudy DB record, then runs phases sequentially. Knows total_phases and total_trials_planned from resolve_study_phase_totals().

TRAINING_REQUESTED:  experiment_id
TRAINING_STARTED:    experiment_id, total_phases, total_trials_planned, resume
TRAINING_PROGRESS:   experiment_id, phases_completed, total_phases, trials_completed, total_trials_planned, progress_percent
TRAINING_DONE:       experiment_id, phases_completed, total_phases, trials_completed, total_trials_planned, status, duration_seconds, best_trial_id?, best_score?

Why each field:

  • experiment_id — Always required. Training always runs for an experiment.
  • total_phases — From resolve_study_phase_totals().phases_total. Fixed at creation. Never changes.
  • total_trials_planned — Sum of n_trials across all phases. From resolve_study_phase_totals().trials_planned. Known at start.
  • resume — True if --resume flag was passed (continuing from checkpoint). Always known (defaults to False).
  • phases_completed — How many phases finished so far.
  • trials_completed — Total trials completed across all phases so far.
  • status — Final outcome: "completed" / "failed" / "cancelled".
  • best_trial_id?, best_score? — Optional because training may fail before any trial completes.

Changes from v2: Renamed trials_completed_overall → trials_completed and total_trials_overall → total_trials_planned (cleaner, matched to resolve_study_phase_totals() output). Made resume required (always known, defaults False). Removed starting_phase? (if resuming, the starting phase is internal detail). Added total_trials_planned to STARTED (known from phase resolution). Added trials_completed and total_trials_planned to DONE (final tally). Replaced completed_phases with phases_completed (consistent verb form).

docs.title: G. Phase (nested inside Training)
docs.text:
Process: One Optuna optimization run within a training session. Creates/resumes an Optuna study with n_trials trials. Uses data_fraction to control how much training data trials see (progressive training). Can warm-start from previous phase's best trials.

PHASE_STARTED:   experiment_id, study_id, phase_number, total_phases, planned_trials, data_fraction
PHASE_PROGRESS:  experiment_id, study_id, phase_number, total_phases, trials_completed, planned_trials, progress_percent,
                 current_trial_id?, current_trial_number?, last_trial_outcome?, last_trial_score?
PHASE_DONE:      experiment_id, study_id, phase_number, total_phases, n_completed, n_pruned, n_failed, planned_trials,
                 best_trial_id?, best_score?, duration_seconds

Why each field:

  • experiment_id — Always required.
  • study_id — OptunaStudy DB primary key. Created before the phase starts. Uniquely identifies this optimization run.
  • phase_number — Which phase in the sequence (1, 2, 3...). From PhaseConfig.phase_number.
  • total_phases — Total phases in this training run. Always known (from training-level resolution).
  • planned_trials — How many trials this phase will run (PhaseConfig.n_trials). Fixed at phase start.
  • data_fraction — The defining parameter of progressive training (e.g., 0.3 = 30% of data). From PhaseConfig.data_fraction. This is what makes phase 1 different from phase 2.
  • trials_completed — Trials finished in this phase so far (completed + pruned + failed).
  • n_completed, n_pruned, n_failed — Breakdown by outcome. From OptunaStudy counters.
  • current_trial_id?, current_trial_number? — Optional because between trials there's no "current" one.
  • last_trial_outcome? — "completed" / "pruned" / "failed". Optional because first PROGRESS before any trial finishes has no last outcome.
  • last_trial_score? — Score of the last finished trial. Optional (pruned/failed trials may not have scores).
  • best_trial_id?, best_score? — Optional because all trials may fail.
  • duration_seconds — Required on DONE. Phase always has a measurable duration.

Changes from v2: Added data_fraction to STARTED (THE key progressive training parameter — you can't understand a phase without knowing its data fraction). Added planned_trials to DONE (for final tally context). Made duration_seconds required on DONE (always measurable). Removed resumed_from_checkpoint? (internal detail, warm-start info is not needed in events).

docs.title: H. Trial (nested inside Phase)
docs.text:
Process: One Optuna trial. Samples hyperparameters (or inherits from parent trial if warm-starting), builds SMALL windows, trains a model for total_epochs epochs, evaluates through 3 stages (train → validation → mini-backtest), reports objective value. Each epoch produces EpochSnapshotMetrics.

TRIAL_STARTED:   experiment_id, study_id, trial_id, trial_number, phase_number
TRIAL_PROGRESS:  experiment_id, study_id, trial_id, trial_number, phase_number,
                 epoch, total_epochs, progress_percent,
                 train_loss, val_loss, f1_macro, epoch_objective_value,
                 val_accuracy?, recall?, precision?, entry_count?, entry_f1?, best_score_so_far?
TRIAL_DONE:      experiment_id, study_id, trial_id, trial_number, phase_number,
                 outcome, duration_seconds,
                 score?, reason?, step_pruned_at?, error_message?, error_type?

Why each field:

  • experiment_id, study_id, trial_id — The full ID chain. Always required. Trial always exists inside a study inside an experiment.
  • trial_number — Optuna's trial sequence number (0-indexed per study). If phase 1 has 10 trials and phase 2 has 7, phase 2 trials are numbered 10–16.
  • phase_number — Which phase this trial belongs to. Always known.
  • epoch / total_epochs — Current epoch (1-indexed) and total. From training loop.
  • train_loss, val_loss — Always computed every epoch. From EpochSnapshotMetrics.
  • f1_macro — Always computed. Primary classification metric.
  • epoch_objective_value — The objective function value for this epoch (what Optuna optimizes). Always computed.
  • val_accuracy? — Optional because some model configurations may not compute it.
  • recall?, precision? — Optional for same reason.
  • entry_count?, entry_f1? — Entry-specific metrics. Optional because they depend on prediction distribution (if model predicts all NONE, these are zero/undefined).
  • best_score_so_far? — Best epoch objective seen so far in this trial. Optional (may not be tracked in all configurations).
  • outcome — Literal["completed", "pruned", "failed", "cancelled"]. Always known when trial ends.
  • score? — Final objective value. Optional because pruned/failed trials may not produce one.
  • reason? — Why the trial ended (e.g., pruning reason, cancellation reason). Optional for completed trials.
  • step_pruned_at? — Epoch at which pruner triggered. Only for pruned trials.
  • error_message?, error_type? — Only for failed trials.

Changes from v2: Renamed loss → train_loss (disambiguating — there's also val_loss). Removed entry_rate_over_total_predictions? (too granular for events — belongs in DB/logs). Removed is_recoverable? from DONE (internal error handling detail, not useful for event consumers).

docs.title: I. Backtest
docs.text:
Process: Simulates trading using a trained model over a historical range. Three types: MINI (during trial, last N rows from SMALL), COMPLETE (after phase, full range), MANUAL (user CLI). Creates a BacktestSession DB record. Runs tick-by-tick: model predicts, bot decides, orders execute with slippage/commission.

BACKTEST_REQUESTED:  experiment_id, study_id?, trial_id, backtest_type
BACKTEST_STARTED:    experiment_id, study_id?, trial_id, backtest_session_id, backtest_type,
                     range_start, range_end, initial_equity
BACKTEST_PROGRESS:   experiment_id, study_id?, trial_id, backtest_session_id, backtest_type,
                     processed_timestamps, total_timestamps, progress_percent,
                     equity?, open_positions?, closed_trades_count?
BACKTEST_DONE:       experiment_id, study_id?, trial_id, backtest_session_id, backtest_type,
                     outcome, duration_seconds,
                     total_pnl, return_pct, sharpe_ratio, trades_count, win_rate,
                     winning_trades?, losing_trades?, avg_win?, avg_loss?,
                     equity?, unrealized_pnl?, equity_mark_to_market?,
                     benchmark_return?, alpha?, prediction_health?

Why each field:

  • experiment_id — Always required. Backtest always runs for an experiment.
  • study_id? — Optional. MINI/COMPLETE backtests inside training have it. MANUAL backtests from CLI may not.
  • trial_id — Always required. BacktestSession always has trial_id (you're backtesting a specific trial's model).
  • backtest_session_id — DB primary key. Created at start. Required from STARTED onwards (multiple sessions per trial possible).
  • backtest_type — "MINI" / "COMPLETE" / "MANUAL". Required across ALL events in the family. Always known at request time. You always know WHAT KIND of backtest you're requesting.
  • range_start, range_end — The historical range being simulated. Known at start.
  • initial_equity — Starting equity (default 10,000). From config.
  • outcome — "completed" / "failed". Required on DONE (backtest always has a final status).
  • total_pnl, return_pct, sharpe_ratio, trades_count, win_rate — The 5 key metrics. Required because even a failed backtest produces partial metrics (or zeroes).
  • Optional metrics — The full BacktestMetrics detail. Present when backtest completes normally.
  • prediction_health? — PredictionHealthMetrics (model prediction quality analysis). Optional, present when enabled.

Changes from v2: Added backtest_type to ALL events (always known, critical for understanding what's happening). Added study_id? to ALL events (optional — manual backtests don't have it). Added outcome to DONE (replaces the weird pruned: bool — consistent with Trial pattern). Removed checkpoint_selection_mode?/checkpoint_selection_epoch? from STARTED (internal model-loading detail). Removed pruned/pruning_reason? (subsumed by outcome). Removed processed_timestamps?/total_timestamps? from DONE (already in PROGRESS, DONE tells outcome not position).

docs.title: J. Live Trading
docs.text:
Process: Runs a live trading session from an ExecutionPlan. Each plan defines one symbol+timeframe+model combination. The runner spawns a LiveInstance, creates a LiveSession DB record, subscribes to a candle stream, and runs the bot loop: receive candle → model predicts → bot decides → execute orders.

ExecutionPlan always carries: experiment_id, trial_id, mode, target_symbol, target_timeframe.

LIVE_TRADING_REQUESTED:          execution_plan_id, execution_plan_uuid, experiment_id, trial_id,
                                 mode, target_symbol, target_timeframe
LIVE_TRADING_STARTED:            execution_plan_id, execution_plan_uuid, experiment_id, trial_id, live_session_id,
                                 mode, target_symbol, target_timeframe
LIVE_TRADING_PROGRESS:           execution_plan_id, execution_plan_uuid, experiment_id, trial_id, live_session_id,
                                 target_symbol, target_timeframe,
                                 equity, realized_pnl_total, unrealized_pnl_total,
                                 open_positions_count, closed_trades_count, executed_orders_count,
                                 decision_id?, candle_id?
LIVE_TRADING_DONE:               execution_plan_id, execution_plan_uuid, experiment_id, trial_id, live_session_id,
                                 reason, duration_seconds
LIVE_TRADING_ANOMALY_DETECTED:   execution_plan_id, execution_plan_uuid, experiment_id, trial_id, live_session_id,
                                 target_symbol, target_timeframe,
                                 anomaly_type, details, severity?, recommended_action?

Why each field:

  • execution_plan_id — DB primary key of ExecutionPlan. Primary routing ID for the live family.
  • execution_plan_uuid — Stable UUID that survives session restarts. Required across ALL events (always available from the ExecutionPlan record).
  • experiment_id, trial_id — From ExecutionPlanConfig. Always present (live trading always runs a specific experiment's trial). Required across ALL events.
  • live_session_id — LiveSession DB primary key. Created at spawn time. Required from STARTED onwards. One ExecutionPlan can have many sessions (restarts, retries).
  • mode — "LIVE_SIM" / "LIVE_REAL". Always known. Changed from optional in some events to required in REQUESTED and STARTED (you always know the mode).
  • target_symbol, target_timeframe — The trading pair and candle timeframe. From ExecutionPlanConfig. Required where relevant (REQUESTED, STARTED, PROGRESS, ANOMALY). Consistent naming throughout (not symbol/timeframe in some and target_symbol/target_timeframe in others).
  • equity, realized_pnl_total, unrealized_pnl_total — Live P&L state. From InstanceManager tick loop.
  • open_positions_count, closed_trades_count, executed_orders_count — Trading activity counters.
  • decision_id? — Globally unique ID for one bot decision on one candle (live:{plan_id}:{session_id}:{candle_ts_ms}). Optional because PROGRESS may fire before any decision is made.
  • candle_id? — Source data identifier ({symbol}:{timeframe}:{ts_ms}). Optional for same reason.
  • reason — Why the session ended (e.g., "manual_stop", "stream_runtime_failure", "data_gap_auto_pause"). Always present on DONE.
  • duration_seconds — Required on DONE (session always has measurable duration).
  • anomaly_type — "data_gap_auto_pause" / "stream_runtime_failure". From code.
  • details — Human-readable anomaly description.
  • severity?, recommended_action? — Not yet implemented in code. Planned for future.

Changes from v2: Added experiment_id and trial_id to ALL events (always available from ExecutionPlan — they were inexplicably missing). Made execution_plan_uuid required across ALL events (always available). Made duration_seconds required on DONE. Removed scope? from DONE (not implemented, undefined meaning). Consistent target_symbol/target_timeframe naming everywhere (was symbol/timeframe in PROGRESS and ANOMALY). Removed requested_by? from REQUESTED (not in the current ExecutionPlanConfig contract).

docs.title: K. HITL (Human-in-the-Loop)
docs.text:
Process: Governance mechanism for sensitive actions. Partially implemented — payloads and DB audit table defined, but the orchestrator interruption mechanism is not wired yet. Flow: system requests approval → control panel displays → human decides → system proceeds or stops.

HITL_REQUEST:   experiment_id?, trial_id?, action, description, context, allowed_responses
HITL_DECISION:  experiment_id?, trial_id?, action, decision, approver, reason?
HITL_APPROVED:  experiment_id?, trial_id?, action, approver, reason?
HITL_REJECTED:  experiment_id?, trial_id?, action, approver, reason?

Why each field:

  • experiment_id?, trial_id? — Optional. HITL gates may apply to non-experiment actions (e.g., budget extensions, master plan updates). When triggered from within a trial, both are present. IDs always go first.
  • action — What's being gated (e.g., "update_master_plan", "experiment_create", "budget_extension"). From HITLRequestPayload.action. Always present — it's the primary key of the governance request.
  • description — Human-readable explanation of what the system wants to do. Always present on REQUEST.
  • context — Dict of contextual data (experiment_id, budget_amount, plan_changes, etc.). Always present on REQUEST.
  • allowed_responses — What the human can choose (default: ["approve", "reject"]). Always present on REQUEST.
  • decision — "approve" / "reject". Always present on DECISION.
  • approver — Who made the decision (user ID / name). Always present on DECISION/APPROVED/REJECTED.
  • reason? — Why the human decided this way. Optional (human may not provide a reason).

Open question: HITL_APPROVED and HITL_REJECTED carry the same semantic as HITL_DECISION with decision="approve"/"reject". Consider consolidating to just HITL_DECISION — same pattern as TRIAL_DONE with outcome.

docs.title: Consistency Matrix
docs.text: Rule Status Evidence Every *_PROGRESS has progress_percent ✅ All 10 PROGRESS events verified IDs always appear first in every payload ✅ All 45 events verified ID optionality is consistent within each family ✅ experiment_id? same in all Data Fetch; study_id? same in all Backtest; etc. *_REQUESTED only has fields the requester actually passes ✅ No speculative fields in REQUESTED *_STARTED adds only newly-created IDs and validated parameters ✅ session_id, study_id, etc. appear first in STARTED *_DONE has IDs + outcome + key results (not config repeat) ✅ No config duplication in DONE events Every trial-level event has experiment_id, study_id, trial_id, trial_number, phase_number ✅ Trial family H verified Every phase-level event has experiment_id, study_id, phase_number, total_phases ✅ Phase family G verified Every training-level event has experiment_id ✅ Training family F verified Every backtest event has experiment_id, trial_id, backtest_type ✅ Backtest family I verified Every live event has execution_plan_id, execution_plan_uuid, experiment_id, trial_id ✅ Live family J verified Field names consistent within family ✅ target_symbol/target_timeframe consistent in Live; features_* consistent in Data Processing No payload duplicates experiment config content ✅ Only IDs + what changed/produced

docs.title: Open Questions for Manu
docs.text:
1. HITL consolidation: Keep 3 events (HITL_DECISION, HITL_APPROVED, HITL_REJECTED) or consolidate to just HITL_DECISION with decision field? Same pattern as TRIAL_DONE with outcome.
  2. study_id naming: Used study_id (OptunaStudy DB PK). Alternatives: optuna_study_id, study_db_id. Preference?
  3. TRIAL_PROGRESS required metrics: train_loss, val_loss, f1_macro, epoch_objective_value as required. Rest optional. Right cut?
  4. BACKTEST_DONE required metrics: total_pnl, return_pct, sharpe_ratio, trades_count, win_rate as required. Right cut?

docs.title: Event Taxonomy Refactor
docs.text: Status: 🔵 STAGE 3 — Task Planning Started: 2026-03-19 Priority: HIGH

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text:
Refactor the event system to establish a coherent, consistent, and logically correct taxonomy across all modules. The current event enum contains broken naming patterns, redundant events, and missing events. This plan defines the approved taxonomy and derives all implementation tasks from it.

Predecessor audit: ./BRAIN_FOUNDATION_AUDIT_PLAN.md (Stage 1 completed)

Approved taxonomy (user-confirmed 2026-03-19):

Training (container)
  └── Phase (optimization phase within a training run)
        └── Trial (single Optuna iteration within a phase)
              └── Epoch (training loop iteration within a trial)

Cascade rule: Every epoch emits events at all 3 levels. Every trial-end emits at 2 levels. Every phase-end emits at 1 level.

Trigger Events emitted Epoch completes TRIAL_PROGRESS + PHASE_PROGRESS + TRAINING_PROGRESS Trial completes TRIAL_DONE + PHASE_PROGRESS + TRAINING_PROGRESS Phase completes PHASE_DONE + TRAINING_PROGRESS

Normalization decisions locked on 2026-03-20:

  • Batch workflows: Use _REQUESTED → _STARTED → _PROGRESS → _DONE.
  • Live trading: It follows the same naming convention as the other modules. Its normalized family is LIVE_TRADING_REQUESTED → LIVE_TRADING_STARTED → LIVE_TRADING_PROGRESS → LIVE_TRADING_DONE, plus LIVE_TRADING_ANOMALY_DETECTED for safety incidents.
  • Heartbeat rename: LIVE_EXEC_SUMMARY is renamed to LIVE_TRADING_PROGRESS.
  • Inventory source: The keep/rename/delete/new decisions in this plan are lowered from BRAIN_FOUNDATION_AUDIT_PLAN.md section F16.B.

docs.title: Global rules (do not remove)
docs.text:
• README template: All module READMEs must follow docs/README_MODULE_TEMPLATE.md.
  • Read from disk before each iteration: Before starting work, re-open this plan, the relevant docs, and the code you will touch.
  • Git workflow: Use the MCP GitHub tools and follow docs/GIT-COMMITS.md. Never run git add from the repository root unless explicitly specified in this plan.
  • Respect project docs: All changes must comply with the relevant docs.
  • Language: All code, docs, and plan text in English.

docs.title: Project docs (must be re-opened when relevant)
docs.text: Document Description BRAIN_ARCHITECTURE_ANALYSIS.md Phase A architecture baseline, especially live-trading section 5.5 BRAIN_FOUNDATION_AUDIT_PLAN.md Audit findings and full gap inventory F16 CODE-GENERATION.md Coding standards, quality gates CONFIGURATION.md Config system and shared BaseConfig rules EVENTS.md Event types and Event Bridge — primary output of this plan MASTER-PLAN.md Master Plan — contains event references WORK-PLANS.md Work plan creation workflow

docs.title: Module READMEs (must be consulted when touching a module)
docs.text: Module README common README.md model-trainer README.md backtesting-engine README.md data-processing README.md data-fetcher README.md housekeeping README.md labeling-service README.md live-trading-runner README.md window-preparer README.md

docs.title: Plan-specific rules
docs.text:
• Enum is source of truth: modules/common/src/rainbow/common/types/events.py is the single source of truth for EventType. All docs derive from it.
  • No compat aliases: Do not create compatibility aliases for deleted event names. Rename directly — all callsites must be updated in the same PR.
  • Payload catalog: The target payload catalog for the final canonical set is part of this plan and becomes the contract implementation must follow.
  • Payload schemas required: Every emitted event must have a Pydantic payload model in EVENT_PAYLOAD_MAP. Raw dict payloads are not acceptable for emitted events.
  • Callsite audit before rename: Before renaming any EventType, grep all modules for its current name to find every callsite. Update all of them.
  • Tests must reflect new names: Any test that references a deleted or renamed EventType must be updated.

docs.title: Finding 1: Training→Phase→Trial hierarchy missing from event system
docs.text:
• Problem: The event system has no PHASE_* events and no TRAINING_DONE event. The existing ALL_TRIALS_DONE event actually represents end-of-phase (it has phase_number in payload), not end-of-training. The hierarchy Training→Phase→Trial is not reflected in the event taxonomy.
  • Location:
  • modules/common/src/rainbow/common/types/events.py — EventType enum, missing PHASE_STARTED/PROGRESS/DONE, TRAINING_DONE
  • modules/model-trainer/src/rainbow/model_trainer/helpers/optuna/trials.py:945 — emits ALL_TRIALS_DONE with phase_number in payload
  • Impact: The orchestrator cannot distinguish end-of-phase from end-of-training. No event signals that run_optimization() completed all phases.
  • Recommendation: Add PHASE_STARTED, PHASE_PROGRESS, PHASE_DONE (replacing ALL_TRIALS_DONE). Add TRAINING_DONE. Keep TRAINING_STARTED (already declared, not emitted).
  • User Decision: Approved 2026-03-19. Taxonomy confirmed: Training→Phase→Trial with cascade progress events.
  • Action Required: Add PHASE_STARTED, PHASE_PROGRESS, PHASE_DONE to enum. Rename ALL_TRIALS_DONE → PHASE_DONE. Implement emission in model-trainer.

docs.title: Finding 2: TRIAL_COMPLETED/PRUNED/FAILED must consolidate into TRIAL_DONE
docs.text:
• Problem: Three separate terminal events for a trial (TRIAL_COMPLETED, TRIAL_PRUNED, TRIAL_FAILED) break the *_DONE convention. The outcome (completed/pruned/failed/cancelled) belongs in the payload, not in the event name.
  • Location:
  • modules/common/src/rainbow/common/types/events.py — three separate EventType values
  • modules/model-trainer/src/rainbow/model_trainer/helpers/optuna/objective.py:635 — emits TRIAL_COMPLETED
  • modules/model-trainer/src/rainbow/model_trainer/helpers/optuna/objective.py:696 — emits TRIAL_PRUNED
  • modules/model-trainer/src/rainbow/model_trainer/helpers/optuna/objective.py:816 — emits TRIAL_FAILED
  • Impact: Inconsistent with the *_DONE convention. Orchestrator must listen to 3 events instead of 1.
  • Recommendation: Delete TRIAL_COMPLETED, TRIAL_PRUNED, TRIAL_FAILED. Add TRIAL_DONE with payload outcome: Literal["completed", "pruned", "failed", "cancelled"].
  • User Decision: Approved 2026-03-19. Single TRIAL_DONE with outcome in payload.
  • Action Required: Replace 3 enum values with TRIAL_DONE. Update all 3 emit callsites in objective.py. Add Pydantic schema TrialDonePayload.

docs.title: Finding 3: TRIAL_PROGRESS missing (cascade rule)
docs.text:
• Problem: TRIAL_PROGRESS is not declared in the enum. Per the cascade rule, every epoch completion must emit TRIAL_PROGRESS (among others).
  • Location: modules/common/src/rainbow/common/types/events.py — missing enum value
  • Impact: Orchestrator cannot observe per-epoch progress at the trial level.
  • Recommendation: Add TRIAL_PROGRESS to enum. Emit it from training_processing.py alongside the existing TRAINING_PROGRESS.
  • User Decision: Approved 2026-03-19.
  • Action Required: Add TRIAL_PROGRESS to enum with Pydantic schema. Emit from training_processing.py at each epoch.

docs.title: Finding 4: BACKTEST_TRIAL_DONE must be renamed to BACKTEST_DONE
docs.text:
• Problem: BACKTEST_TRIAL_DONE breaks the {OPERATION}_DONE naming convention. The word TRIAL in the middle is a hybrid between operation and container names.
  • Location:
  • modules/common/src/rainbow/common/types/events.py — BACKTEST_TRIAL_DONE
  • modules/backtesting-engine/src/rainbow/backtesting_engine/helpers/engine_loop.py:1313 — emit callsite
  • Impact: Inconsistent naming. No BACKTEST_STARTED/PROGRESS family exists.
  • Recommendation: Rename BACKTEST_TRIAL_DONE → BACKTEST_DONE. Add missing BACKTEST_STARTED, BACKTEST_PROGRESS (declared but not emitted — can stay deferred).
  • User Decision: Approved 2026-03-19.
  • Action Required: Rename in enum. Update callsite in engine_loop.py. Add Pydantic schema BacktestDonePayload.

docs.title: Finding 5: TRIAL_TRAINING_DONE must be deleted
docs.text:
• Problem: TRIAL_TRAINING_DONE is a hybrid name mixing container (Trial) and operation (Training). It has no callsite — it is declared but never emitted. The concept it represents is now covered by TRIAL_DONE (trial completed its training = trial done).
  • Location: modules/common/src/rainbow/common/types/events.py — declared, no callsite
  • Impact: Dead code creating conceptual confusion.
  • Recommendation: Delete from enum. No replacement needed — TRIAL_DONE covers this.
  • User Decision: Approved 2026-03-19.
  • Action Required: Delete TRIAL_TRAINING_DONE from enum and EVENT_PAYLOAD_MAP.

docs.title: Finding 6: DATA_PROCESSING family missing — data-processing emits wrong event
docs.text:
• Problem: The data-processing module emits DATA_FETCH_DONE (wrong event — DATA_FETCH_DONE belongs to data-fetcher). The module has no own normalized family. Two legacy events exist: DATA_PROCESSED (marked deprecated) and DATA_PROCESSED_DONE (declared, never emitted). The family is also missing DATA_PROCESSING_PROGRESS.
  • Location:
  • modules/data-processing/src/rainbow/data_processing/data_processing.py:882,1654,1853 — emits DATA_FETCH_DONE erroneously
  • modules/common/src/rainbow/common/types/events.py — DATA_PROCESSED (legacy), DATA_PROCESSED_DONE (unused)
  • Impact: data-processing completion is invisible and masquerades as data-fetch completion.
  • Recommendation: Delete DATA_PROCESSED (legacy). Rename DATA_PROCESSED_DONE → DATA_PROCESSING_DONE. Add DATA_PROCESSING_STARTED and DATA_PROCESSING_PROGRESS. Fix the 3 callsites in data_processing.py to emit DATA_PROCESSING_DONE, and add throttled DATA_PROCESSING_PROGRESS for dense processing loops.
  • User Decision: Approved 2026-03-19.
  • Action Required: Delete DATA_PROCESSED. Rename DATA_PROCESSED_DONE → DATA_PROCESSING_DONE. Add DATA_PROCESSING_STARTED and DATA_PROCESSING_PROGRESS. Fix 3 callsites and add throttled progress emission.

docs.title: Finding 7: DATA_PIPELINE events must be deleted
docs.text:
• Problem: DATA_PIPELINE_REQUESTED and DATA_PIPELINE_DONE exist without a STARTED or PROGRESS counterpart, and without any callsite. The concept of a "pipeline" event is redundant — the orchestrator coordinates the 4 individual data operations via their own *_DONE events.
  • Location: modules/common/src/rainbow/common/types/events.py — two declared, zero callsites
  • Impact: Dead declarations creating ambiguity about system responsibilities.
  • Recommendation: Delete both. The orchestrator uses DATA_FETCH_DONE, LABELS_DONE, FAT_WINDOWS_DONE, SMALL_WINDOWS_DONE directly.
  • User Decision: Approved 2026-03-19.
  • Action Required: Delete DATA_PIPELINE_REQUESTED and DATA_PIPELINE_DONE from enum.

docs.title: Finding 8: Live family must fully align with the shared module naming convention
docs.text:
• Problem: The current live-trading family does not follow the normalized naming pattern used elsewhere in the plan. LIVE_EXEC_SUMMARY is functionally the live heartbeat/progress event, but its name hides that role. LIVE_TRADING_STOPPED breaks the terminal _DONE convention, the family is missing LIVE_TRADING_REQUESTED, and LIVE_ANOMALY_DETECTED does not share the stable LIVE_TRADING_ prefix.
  • Location:
  • modules/live-trading-runner/src/rainbow/live_trading_runner/live_trading_runner.py:242,275,284 — raw dict
  • modules/live-trading-runner/src/rainbow/live_trading_runner/helpers/instance_manager.py:356,573,699 — raw dict
  • Impact: Inconsistent naming inside the same family. Live trading would remain the only module whose request/start/progress/done lifecycle does not share a single stable prefix.
  • Recommendation: Normalize the live family as LIVE_TRADING_REQUESTED → LIVE_TRADING_STARTED → LIVE_TRADING_PROGRESS → LIVE_TRADING_DONE, plus LIVE_TRADING_ANOMALY_DETECTED. Rename LIVE_EXEC_SUMMARY → LIVE_TRADING_PROGRESS.
  • User Decision: Approved 2026-03-20.
  • Action Required: Add LIVE_TRADING_REQUESTED. Rename LIVE_EXEC_SUMMARY → LIVE_TRADING_PROGRESS, LIVE_TRADING_STOPPED → LIVE_TRADING_DONE, and LIVE_ANOMALY_DETECTED → LIVE_TRADING_ANOMALY_DETECTED in enum, callsites, docs, and MODULE_EVENT_MAP. Add LiveTradingRequestedPayload, LiveTradingStartedPayload, LiveTradingProgressPayload, LiveTradingDonePayload, LiveTradingAnomalyDetectedPayload.

docs.title: Finding 9: Progress families need schemas and explicit throttling rules
docs.text:
• Problem: Three *_PROGRESS events are emitted with raw dict payloads but have no Pydantic schema registered in EVENT_PAYLOAD_MAP: TRAINING_PROGRESS, DATA_FETCH_PROGRESS, SMALL_WINDOWS_PROGRESS. BACKTEST_PROGRESS is declared but still lacks an implementation decision in this plan. DATA_PROCESSING_PROGRESS must be added. The plan also did not state which modules need throttled progress emission and which ones do not.
  • Location:
  • modules/model-trainer/src/rainbow/model_trainer/helpers/model/training_processing.py:544 — TRAINING_PROGRESS raw dict
  • modules/data-fetcher/src/rainbow/data_fetcher/data_fetcher.py:791 — DATA_FETCH_PROGRESS raw dict
  • modules/window-preparer/src/rainbow/window_preparer/small_window_preparer.py:495 — SMALL_WINDOWS_PROGRESS raw dict
  • Impact: Schema validation gap remains, progress semantics stay inconsistent across modules, and dense loops risk flooding the database without a shared throttling policy.
  • Recommendation: Add Pydantic payload models for TRAINING_PROGRESS, DATA_FETCH_PROGRESS, SMALL_WINDOWS_PROGRESS, BACKTEST_PROGRESS, and DATA_PROCESSING_PROGRESS. Introduce one shared EventsConfig(BaseConfig) with progress_emit_interval_seconds and use it only where throttling is needed: backtesting, live_trading_runner, data_processing, window_preparer. Do not add throttle config to model_trainer (epoch-based) or data_fetcher (stream/event semantics kept as-is).
  • User Decision: Approved 2026-03-20. Shared EventsConfig only where needed; no hardcoded cadence constants.
  • Action Required: Add the missing payload models, define shared EventsConfig in common, wire it as a sub-config in the affected modules, and document the cadence in YAML/README/CONFIGURATION docs.

docs.title: Finding 10: HOUSEKEEPING_DONE emitted without schema
docs.text:
• Problem: HOUSEKEEPING_DONE is emitted from housekeeping.py:99 with a raw dict but has no Pydantic schema in EVENT_PAYLOAD_MAP.
  • Location: modules/housekeeping/src/rainbow/housekeeping/housekeeping.py:99
  • Impact: Minor from a typing perspective, but the bigger problem is that the event itself does not belong to a coherent canonical lifecycle.
  • Recommendation: Remove HOUSEKEEPING_DONE from the final canonical taxonomy. If the team still wants it as a housekeeping-only observability event later, add a schema then — but do not treat it as part of the core business taxonomy.
  • User Decision: Superseded on 2026-03-20 by the stricter canonical-flow rule.
  • Action Required: Do not include HOUSEKEEPING_DONE in the final canonical event set. If retained temporarily in code, document it as non-canonical observability debt.

docs.title: Finding 11: Documentation update
docs.text:
• Problem: Both docs reference event names that will change: TRIAL_COMPLETED, BACKTEST_TRIAL_DONE, ALL_TRIALS_DONE. They also do not document the new Training→Phase→Trial hierarchy.
  • Location:
  • docs/EVENTS.md — full event reference doc
  • docs/MASTER-PLAN.md — sections 4.5 (model-trainer), 4.7 (backtesting-engine)
  • Impact: Documentation drift post-refactor.
  • Recommendation: Update both docs after enum changes are complete.
  • User Decision: Pending.
  • Action Required: Update EVENTS.md and MASTER-PLAN.md after all enum/code changes done.

docs.title: Finding 12: Approved events need a complete payload catalog, not just schema placeholders
docs.text:
• Problem: The plan currently does not define the exact payload contract for every event that survives the final canonical taxonomy. That leaves room for ambiguity in required fields, optional fields, minimal payloads, and which events share the same shape.
  • Location:
  • modules/common/src/rainbow/common/types/events.py — EVENT_PAYLOAD_MAP and payload models
  • docs/EVENTS.md — event reference and payload documentation
  • All emitting modules — model-trainer, backtesting-engine, data-processing, data-fetcher, window-preparer, live-trading-runner, housekeeping
  • Impact: Without a full canonical-event catalog, implementation can drift, schemas can remain partial, and documentation can still lag behind the code even if the execution families are already defined.
  • Recommendation: Define the final payload catalog directly in this plan for every event that remains in the canonical set, grouped by family, with exact required fields, optional fields, and shared base structures.
  • User Decision: Approved 2026-03-20.
  • Action Required: Implement the payload models exactly as specified in the catalog below only for events that remain after pruning to the canonical set.

docs.title: 🎯 Final Canonical Flows To Keep
docs.text: This section is now the authoritative target taxonomy.

docs.title: Canonical design rule
docs.text:
• Only keep events that belong to a real domain flow.
  • A real flow must answer four questions: who requests it, who executes/emits it, who consumes it, and what the terminal event is.
  • Default lifecycle for module jobs: *_REQUESTED → *_STARTED → *_PROGRESS → *_DONE.
  • Allowed exceptions:
  • *_ANOMALY_DETECTED as a live-safety side-channel.
  • HITL_* as a human approval flow.
  • Disallowed in the final canonical set: orphan *_READY, *_UPDATED, *_STOPPING, or single *_DONE events without a clear requester/executor/consumer contract.
  • Observability and telemetry are not business taxonomy. If we keep them, they must live in a separate observability spec, not in the canonical execution flow.

docs.title: A. Canonical business flows
docs.text:
• Data fetch
  • Requested by: GRAPH-ORCHESTRATOR
  • Emitted by: GRAPH-ORCHESTRATOR emits REQUESTED; DATA-FETCHER emits execution events
  • Consumed by: GRAPH-ORCHESTRATOR, UI, event bridge
  • Final target events: DATA_FETCH_REQUESTED, DATA_FETCH_STARTED, DATA_FETCH_PROGRESS, DATA_FETCH_DONE
  • Status: Keep
  • Labels
  • Requested by: GRAPH-ORCHESTRATOR
  • Emitted by: GRAPH-ORCHESTRATOR + LABELING-SERVICE
  • Consumed by: GRAPH-ORCHESTRATOR, UI, event bridge
  • Final target events: LABELS_REQUESTED, LABELS_STARTED, LABELS_PROGRESS, LABELS_DONE
  • Status: Keep
  • FAT windows
  • Requested by: GRAPH-ORCHESTRATOR
  • Emitted by: GRAPH-ORCHESTRATOR + WINDOW-PREPARER
  • Consumed by: GRAPH-ORCHESTRATOR, UI, event bridge
  • Final target events: FAT_WINDOWS_REQUESTED, FAT_WINDOWS_STARTED, FAT_WINDOWS_PROGRESS, FAT_WINDOWS_DONE
  • Status: Keep
  • SMALL windows
  • Requested by: GRAPH-ORCHESTRATOR
  • Emitted by: GRAPH-ORCHESTRATOR + WINDOW-PREPARER
  • Consumed by: GRAPH-ORCHESTRATOR, UI, event bridge
  • Final target events: SMALL_WINDOWS_REQUESTED, SMALL_WINDOWS_STARTED, SMALL_WINDOWS_PROGRESS, SMALL_WINDOWS_DONE
  • Status: Keep
  • Data processing
  • Requested by: GRAPH-ORCHESTRATOR
  • Emitted by: GRAPH-ORCHESTRATOR + DATA-PROCESSING
  • Consumed by: GRAPH-ORCHESTRATOR, UI, event bridge
  • Final target events: DATA_PROCESSING_REQUESTED, DATA_PROCESSING_STARTED, DATA_PROCESSING_PROGRESS, DATA_PROCESSING_DONE
  • Status: Keep as target flow; current code is inconsistent and still uses deprecated DATA_PROCESSED* instead
  • Training
  • Requested by: GRAPH-ORCHESTRATOR
  • Emitted by: GRAPH-ORCHESTRATOR + MODEL-TRAINER
  • Consumed by: GRAPH-ORCHESTRATOR, UI, event bridge
  • Final target events: TRAINING_REQUESTED, TRAINING_STARTED, TRAINING_PROGRESS, TRAINING_DONE
  • Status: Keep
  • Phase (nested inside training)
  • Requested by: no separate requester; emitted inside TRAINING_* execution
  • Emitted by: MODEL-TRAINER
  • Consumed by: UI, orchestration logic
  • Final target events: PHASE_STARTED, PHASE_PROGRESS, PHASE_DONE
  • Status: Keep
  • Trial (nested inside training)
  • Requested by: no separate requester; emitted inside TRAINING_* execution
  • Emitted by: MODEL-TRAINER
  • Consumed by: UI, orchestration logic, backtest scheduler
  • Final target events: TRIAL_STARTED, TRIAL_PROGRESS, TRIAL_DONE
  • Status: Keep
  • Backtest
  • Requested by: GRAPH-ORCHESTRATOR
  • Emitted by: GRAPH-ORCHESTRATOR + BACKTESTING-ENGINE
  • Consumed by: GRAPH-ORCHESTRATOR, UI, event bridge
  • Final target events: BACKTEST_REQUESTED, BACKTEST_STARTED, BACKTEST_PROGRESS, BACKTEST_DONE
  • Status: Keep
  • Live trading
  • Requested by: GRAPH-ORCHESTRATOR or control plane
  • Emitted by: GRAPH-ORCHESTRATOR + LIVE-TRADING-RUNNER
  • Consumed by: UI, ops, orchestration logic
  • Final target events: LIVE_TRADING_REQUESTED, LIVE_TRADING_STARTED, LIVE_TRADING_PROGRESS, LIVE_TRADING_DONE, LIVE_TRADING_ANOMALY_DETECTED
  • Status: Keep

docs.title: B. Governance flow kept separately from business execution flows
docs.text:
• Flow: Human approval
  • Requested by: GRAPH-ORCHESTRATOR
  • Emitted by: GRAPH-ORCHESTRATOR emits request; CONTROL-PANEL emits decision
  • Consumed by: GRAPH-ORCHESTRATOR
  • Final target events: HITL_REQUEST, HITL_DECISION, HITL_APPROVED, HITL_REJECTED
  • Status: Keep as separate governance flow
